question: What belongs within the scope of authority and responsibilities of the 2nd referee?

answers.a: To whistle the contact of the ball with an outside object.
answers.b: To whistle the completed block by a back row player.
answers.c: To control the work of the ball retrievers.
answers.d: To authorize a requested substitution.
answers.e: To check the score sheet at the end of the match.

question: The ball crosses the net outside the crossing space into the opponent's free zone. In which cases must the line judge in charge indicate this immediately?

answers.a: It happens during a service.
answers.b: It happens after the first touch of the ball by the (receiving) team.
answers.c: It happens after the second touch of the ball by the (receiving) team.
answers.d: It happens after the third touch of the ball by the (receiving) team.

question: A group of players prevent the opponent's view of the server and service hit during their own serve. Is this considered as a screen?

answers.a: Yes, in any case.
answers.b: No, in no way.
answers.c: Yes, if it prevents the view of the flight path of the ball.

question: In which of the following cases may the ball touch several parts of a player's body consecutively?

answers.a: Without restrictions in receiving the opponent's serve in the technique of the overhand finger pass.
answers.b: Without restrictions on all blocking actions of the opponent's serve.
answers.c: During the reception of the opponent’s serve in the technique of the overhand finger pass, if the multiple contacts occur during the same action.

question: After a rally, the 1st referee decides in favor of team A. The latter immediately makes a substitution (it is the first substitution in this set). Then the referee revises his decision and gives the point to Team B. Team A wants to reverse the substitution. Can the referee allow this?

answers.a: Yes, however, the team will be charged with two substitutions.
answers.b: No.
answers.c: Yes, without further restrictions.

question: A front-row player takes off to a block too early and touches the ball with his head below the top of the net after the opponent's attack while 'coming down'. Is the team still entitled to three hits of the ball afterwards?

answers.a: Yes, in any case.
answers.b: Yes, if part of the player's body (e.g. the hands) is still above the top of the net at the moment the ball touches the head.
answers.c: No, in no way.

question: Player No. 8 is warned with a yellow card for a (minor) misconduct. In the further course of the game, a player of the same team shows a similar misconduct. Which of the following reactions of the 1st referee are possible in this situation?

answers.a: He may in any case give a warning to the player concerned by showing the yellow card.
answers.b: He can issue a penalty (red card) to the player in question.
answers.c: If the player in question is not player no. 8, he may give a warning to him by showing the yellow card.

question: Player No. 9 is penalized for rude conduct (red card). No member of the team has yet been warned with a yellow card. As the game continues, a minor misconduct occurs (for the first time) by a player of the same team. Can this player be warned verbally or by showing the yellow card?

answers.a: Yes, in any case.
answers.b: No, in no way.
answers.c: Yes, if it is not player No. 9.

question: After winning a rally, the team makes a libero replacement. After the whistle for the service, the 1st referee blows the whistle again 8 seconds later, because the service player has not executed the service by then. Is the team allowed to make another libero replacement?

answers.a: Only if the opposing team also makes a libero replacement.
answers.b: No.
answers.c: Yes.

question: Team A makes a libero replacement on court, immediately afterwards Team B makes a substitution, then Team A replaces the libero again. The referees notice this at the time of the second libero replacement. How should they proceed?

answers.a: The second replacement will be tolerated, after the following service the rally will be stopped by whistling and the referee decides a fault of team A.
answers.b: The second replacement will be reversed, Team A receives a sanction for delay.
answers.c: The second replacement will be reversed, without further consequences.

question: A team plays with one libero. The coach declares him unable to play and immediately re-designates a new libero. Is this possible?

answers.a: Only when the acting libero is currently not on the court.
answers.b: Only if the acting libero is currently on the court.
answers.c: Yes, in any case.

question: A team plays with 2 liberos (L1 and L2). Libero L1 is injured and becomse unable to play. Which statements are true for the rest of the match?

answers.a: If Libero L2 gets injured, the team can re-designate a new Libero.
answers.b: If Libero L2 is expelled, the team can re-designate a new Libero.
answers.c: If Libero L2 is disqualified, the team can re-designate a new Libero.
answers.d: Libero L1 is allowed to act as libero again in any case when he has recovered from his injury.

question: In the course of a set the players No. 4 (for No. 3) and No. 6 (for No. 5) were substituted into the match. Then players No. 3 (with substitution paddle No. 4) and No. 5 (with substitution paddle No. 10) appear in the substitution zone, requesting a double substitution. How to proceed?

answers.a: Both substitutions will be rejected. The team receives a sanction for delay.
answers.b: The substitution '3 for 4' is granted, the other substitution is rejected. The match continues without further consequences.
answers.c: The substitution '3 for 4' is granted, the other substitution is rejected. The team receives a sanction for delay.

question: A team requests a double substitution after having already made 5 substitutions during the set. What is the procedure?

answers.a: The 2nd referee asks the coach which one of the two substitutions he wants to make. This one will be granted, the other substitution request will be rejected. The team will receive a sanction for delay in any case.
answers.b: The 2nd referee asks the coach which one of the two substitutions he wants to make. This one will be granted, the other substitution request will be rejected. If there is no delay, the second request is treated as an improper request.
answers.c: Both substitution requests will be rejected. The team will receive a sanction for delay.

question: A player has been replaced by the libero, is sitting on the team bench, and is expelled for unsportsmanlike conduct (the libero is in position 6). What should be done next?

answers.a: The libero may initially remain on the court. Only when he is to leave the court again, a substitute player must be designated for the player who has been expelled and must come onto the court.
answers.b: The player must be substituted immediately outside the court, the libero may remain on the court for the time being.
answers.c: In any case, the player must be immediately replaced with the libero back on the court and then substituted.
answers.d: The player must be substituted immediately outside the court, the new player must immediately replace the libero on the court.

question: The serving team's libero comes onto the court in place of a back-row player, with the replacement action taking place after the whistle for service, but before the ball is hit. This is the second time this has happened in this match. What is the correct reaction of the 1st referee?

answers.a: He allows the rally to be finished and then informs the game captain of the serving team.
answers.b: He whistles and calls a positional fault for the serving team.
answers.c: He shall immediately reject the libero replacement and issue a delay santion on the serving team.
answers.d: He immediately interrupts the rally and issues a delay sanction.

question: Which of these "blocking actions" by a back-row player is/are allowed?

answers.a: The back-row player takes part in an executed collective block, but without touching the ball himself.
answers.b: The libero makes a block attempt.
answers.c: The back-row player reaches with his hands towards the top of the net, but without reaching over the top of the net, and touches the ball below the top of the net.
answers.d: The back-row player makes a block attempt (without touching the ball) and the opponent hits the ball directly "out".

question: A player of the receiving team makes a bad service reception. The ball flies outside the antenna over the vertical plane of the net into the opponent's free zone. Another player of the receiving team runs after the ball and plays it from the opponent's free zone back to his own side of the court. In which situations is this allowed?

answers.a: The ball is returned on the same side of the court between the net and the post without touching the net's cables or ropes.
answers.b: The ball is returned directly over the antenna on the same side of the court.
answers.c: The ball is returned below the net (through the lower space of the net) between the posts and the extension of the antenna.

question: Which of the following situations are within the rules regarding playing the ball over the net?

answers.a: The setter reaches over the top of the net and touches the ball coming from his team in the opponent's space to pass it back to his side of the court.
answers.b: Touching the ball in the opponent's space by a blocking action during the opponent's attack hit.
answers.c: Two opposing front-row players touch the ball simultaneously when it is directly above the top of the net.
answers.d: Touching the ball in the opponent's space before the opponent's attack hit.

question: The team captain of team A is not on the court. After a long rally, the team is not sure who has to serve next. Who is allowed to ask the 2nd referee who will serve next?

answers.a: The coach.
answers.b: The team captain.
answers.c: The assistent coach.
answers.d: The game captain.

question: As team A serves, the ball hits one of its own players in the back. At the same time, the 2nd referee whistles for a positional fault of team B. Which decision(s) is/are correct?

answers.a: Fault of Team B; point and service for Team A.
answers.b: Double fault; Team A serves.
answers.c: Fault of Team A; point and service for Team B.
answers.d: Double fault; Team B serves.

question: Two opposing players touch the ball directly over the net at the same time. Which of the following statements is/are in accordance with the rules?

answers.a: If the ball remains in play afterwards, the team on whose side the ball falls has the right to three touches.
answers.b: If the ball then goes «out» on team A's side, this is a fault for team B.
answers.c: If the two players make contact with the ball for a longer period of time, this is considered a double fault and the rally is repeated.
answers.d: If the ball then touches the antenna, the rally is repeated.

question: An exceptional substitution must be written in the score sheet and counted in the total number of substitutions in that set and in the match. Is this statement correct or incorrect?

answers.a: Incorrect
answers.b: Correct

question: Who is allowed to stay in their own free zone in front of the team bench for a longer period of time during the match?

answers.a: Coach and assistant coach.
answers.b: Coach, assistant coach, team therapist and medical doctor.
answers.c: Coach.
answers.d: Coach, assistant coach and game captain.

question: Player A is the wrong server and executes the service. What should happen?

answers.a: The scorer waits until the rally has been completed and draws the 2nd referee's attention to the fault. The 1st referee must issue a sanction for delay and the opponent is given the next service. If the opponent has won the rally, the opponent also receives a point.
answers.b: The scorer waits until the rally has been completed and draws the 2nd referee's attention to the fault. If the opponent has won the rally, the opponent is awarded a point. Player A's team retains the right to serve and must correct the positions.
answers.c: The scorer waits until the rally has been completed and draws the 2nd referee's attention to the fault. If the opponent has won the rally, the opponent is awarded two points. Player A's team must correct the positions.
answers.d: The scorer waits until the rally has been completed and draws the 2nd referee's attention to the fault. No point is awarded for the rally. The referee whistles and awards a point and service to the opponent.
answers.e: The scorer draws the 2nd referee's attention to the fault and he stops the rally immediately.

question: The libero enters the court for player A. Once on the team bench, player A actes rude towards the referees and receives a penalty. Player A now swears and shouts, whereupon he is expelled and ultimately disqualified. How will the replacement of the libero and the substitution be handled?

answers.a: The libero becomes the substitute for player A and can stay on the court.
answers.b: The libero must leave the court. Player A is substituted for another player on the team bench and no longer physically enters the court. The new player enters the court. The libero can return to the court after the next completed rally.
answers.c: The libero must leave the court. Player A must return to the court to be replaced by the libero.
answers.d: The libero can remain on the court. Player A is legally (if possible, otherwise exceptionally) substituted by another player sitting on the team bench.

question: What is the procedure of the 2nd referee if the line-up sheet of team A does not match with the starting positions on the court?

answers.a: Keeping calm. This is the responsibility of the 1st referee.
answers.b: To go to the coach and ask him what he wants to do.
answers.c: To go to the coach and inform him that his team will receive a sanction for delay.
answers.d: Keeping calm. This is the responsibility of the scorer.

question: Team A has used all possible (legal) substitutions. The libero sits on the team bench. One of the players on the court is expelled. How is the game continued?

answers.a: One of the previously substituted players can be (expectionally) substituted for the expelled player.
answers.b: Team A is allowed to finish the set with five players.
answers.c: The libero can replace the expelled player.
answers.d: Team A loses this set in any case.

question: Player A blocks the ball. The ball hits her face and goes over the antenna into the opponent's free zone. Her team-mate B pursues the ball and also plays it back over the antenna to her own side of the net. What is the correct action of the referees / line judges and why?

answers.a: The ball is "out" (hand signal #15) when it flies over the antenna for the first time.
answers.b: Nothing. This playing of the ball is in accordance with the rules.
answers.c: The ball is "out" (hand signal #15) when it flies back over the antenna.
answers.d: The ball is "out" (hand signal #15) if player B plays the ball in the opponent's free zone.

question: A player enters the substitution zone. A second player (still in a tracksuit) leaves the warm-up area at this moment and runs towards the substitution zone. What should the 2nd referee do at this moment?

answers.a: Allowing only one substitution with the player who is in the substitution zone and impose a delay sanction on the team.
answers.b: Allowing only one substitution with the player who is in the substitution zone.
answers.c: Impose a delay sanction on the team.
answers.d: Allowing a double substitution.
answers.e: Prevent the second player from entering the substitution zone.

question: Three players enter the substitution zone. After the request is recognised and accepted by the scorer, the coach decides to make only two substitutions. What is the correct procedure for the 2nd referee?

answers.a: To execute the two substitions, without sanctions for the teams (as long as there is no delay).
answers.b: To inform the coach that three players need to be substituted.
answers.c: To inform the coach that he needs to take a time-out to change the number of substitutions.
answers.d: Nothing. Substitutions are handled by the scorer, not the referee.

question: Player A is ready to play and enters the substitution zone during an interruption. After the request is recognised and accepted by the scorer, the coach changes her mind and orders the player back to the warm-up area. How should the referees decide?

answers.a: The substitution must be carried out. No sanction.
answers.b: The situation is counted as two substitutions - player A for another player, and then that player for player A. No sanction.
answers.c: The substitution is cancelled. No sanction.
answers.d: The substitution is cancelled and the team receives a sanction for delay.

question: At the change of courts after the 2nd set, player #13 of team A accuses the 1st referee of "being blind" and then shakes the referee's chair violently. How should the 1st referee react?

answers.a: The 1st referee asks the captain of team A to come to him during the interval and explains to him that he will sanction player #13 for aggressive behavior. After the 2nd referee has checked the line-up for the 3rd set, the 1st referee disqualifies player #13 (hand signal #8).
answers.b: The 1st referee asks the coach of team A to come and explains to him that he will sanction player #13 for offensive conduct. The 1st referee then expels player #13 (hand signal #8).
answers.c: After the 2nd referee has checked the line-up for the 3rd set, the 1st referee shows the hand signal for disqualification (hand signal #8) and points to player #13, who is then substituted. Team A receives an additional delay sanction.
answers.d: The 1st referee asks the captain of team A to come to him during the interval and explains to him that he will sanction player #13 for aggressive behavior. After the 2nd referee has checked the line-up for the 3rd set, the 1st referee expels player #13 (hand signal #7).
answers.e: After the 2nd referee has checked the line-up for the 3rd set, the 1st referee shows the hand signal for expulsion (hand signal #7) and points to player #13.

question: Which statement(s) is (are) true?

answers.a: The 1st referee may authorise a serve even if the server is not in the service zone with the ball.
answers.b: If, during the serve, a teammate of the serving player is not completely within the own court (with one foot in the free zone) and the receiving team commits a position fault, a replay must be awarded.
answers.c: Before the service is authorized, the 1st referee must ensure that both teams are ready to play and that the service player is in possession of the ball.
answers.d: In the event of a service fault during the serve and a simultaneous position fault by the receiving team, the first referee shall not decide to replay the game.

question: A team plays with one libero. Which statement(s) is (are) correct?

answers.a: A libero may play the ball, which is not entirely higher than the top of the net, with an overhand finger pass to the opposing team from any position on his side of the playing area.
answers.b: An injured libero receives a recovery time of 3 minutes.
answers.c: The team captain can never be the libero.
answers.d: The coach may re-designate a new libero for an expelled libero.

question: A team has 7 players, one of whom is a libero. One player (position VI) is expelled in the 2nd set. What option(s) does the team have?

answers.a: As the player cannot be substituted, the team is declared incomplete for this set and loses the 2nd set.
answers.b: The coach declares himself to be a playing coach and sustitutes with the sanctioned player.
answers.c: The sanctioned player must be substituted. Therefore, the libero is re-designated as a "normal" player upon request (jersey must be changed!) and the substitution can be carried out.
answers.d: The expelled player can be replaced by the libero. If the libero has to rotate to position IV, the expelled player cannot be substituted legally, the team is then declared incomplete and loses the set.

question: Which fact(s) is (are) entered in the score sheet?

answers.a: An exceptional substitution.
answers.b: A first improper request that did not cause a delay.
answers.c: A formal warning (yellow card).
answers.d: A re-designation of a Libero.

question: After an unsuccessful reception, the ball flies on the own side of the court to the spot marked in red in the diagram. The libero runs to the ball and plays it back into the own side of the court with an overhand finger pass. How must the referees decide?

answers.a: The rally continues unrestricted.
answers.b: The referees stop the rally and decide on ball "out" (hand signal #15).
answers.c: The rally continues until an attack hit of the ball, which was completely higher than the top of the net at the moment of the hit, is completed. Then the rally is stopped and decided as "fault of the attack hit" (hand signal #21).
answers.d: The rally would continue unrestricted even if the pass by the libero had been made from the opponent's free zone (spot marked in blue) – the ball would have entered the opponent's free zone outside the crossing space and would likewise be played back into the own side of the court.

question: A player enters the substitution zone at the moment of the whistle to serve. Which statement is correct?

answers.a: The player's action constitutes an improper request.
answers.b: The player's action is to be sanctioned as a delay in any case.
answers.c: The 2nd referee interrupts the rally and grants the request for a substitution.

question: A coach requests a time-out. The 2nd referee whistles. He is then informed by the scorer that this is the 3rd time-out in this set. The team has not yet made an improper request during the course of the match. What is the procedure?

answers.a: The time-out is cancelled and the game continues without further consequences.
answers.b: The time-out is cancelled. An "improper request" is noted on the score sheet. The game continues without further consequences.
answers.c: The time-out is cancelled and the requesting team is sanctioned for delay.
answers.d: The time-out is cancelled and the requesting team is sanctioned for delay. In addition, an "improper request" is noted on the score sheet.

question: Which statement(s) is (are) true?

answers.a: The playing coach of an NLB team may transfer his rights to the assistant coach for the duration of his active participation in the match.
answers.b: The game captain may request time-outs if the coach is not present. This also applies if the coach has been expelled or disqualified, unless the coach's duties have been taken over by an assistant coach.
answers.c: The coach may ask the 2nd referee for the correctness of the opponent's line-up.
answers.d: During a match, the 2nd referee may replace the sick 1st referee. If no replacement referee can be found, the match can only be continued with the agreement of the team captains.

question: When does the scorer have to click on the 'Start Rally' button in the eScoresheet application?

answers.a: At the moment the 1st referee whistles to authorise the serve.
answers.b: Not at all; the eScoresheet application 'Swiss Volley' does not have this button because Swiss Volley does not use Video Challenge.
answers.c: Between the 1st referee's whistle to authorise the serve and the player hitting the ball.
answers.d: At the moment of the serve (i.e. when the player hits the ball).

question: How many locally formed players (LFP) must be "on the court" during each of the NLA and NLB matches?

answers.a: NLA: 2; NLB: 1
answers.b: NLA: 2; NLB: 3
answers.c: NLA: 3; NLB: 3
answers.d: NLA: 2; NLB: no specification
answers.e: NLA: 2; NLB: 2

question: The team composition must be entered in the 'Roster' section of the eScoresheet application. Which functions can and may be selected for official Swiss Volley competitions in the lower section for the team members on the bench?

answers.a: Scout
answers.b: Medical Doctor
answers.c: Other
answers.d: Assistant
answers.e: Manager

question: Teams can enter one or two libero(s) in the eScoresheet application. These two players are individually LFPs (locally formed players) or not. Which statement(s) is (are) correct?

answers.a: The team has one libero; this player is a LFP. In this team, the libero only counts as a LFP on the court if he is actually on the court.
answers.b: The team has two liberos; both players are not LFPs. In this team, the libero does not count as LFP on the court.
answers.c: The team has two liberos; one of these players is a LFP, the other is not. In this team, the libero does not count as LFP on the court.
answers.d: The team has two liberos; one of these players is a LFP, the other is not. In this team, the libero only counts as LFP on the court if the libero who is also LFP is on the court.
answers.e: The team has one libero; this player is a LFP. In the third set, the libero is injured and another player who is not a LFP is re-designated as the new libero. After the re-designation, the libero continues to count as a LFP on the court in this team.
answers.f: The team has one libero; this player is a LFP. In this team, the libero always counts as a LFP on the court, i.e. even if he is not on the court.
answers.g: The team has one libero; this player is a LFP. In the third set, the libero is injured and another player who is not a LFP is re-designated as the new libero. After the re-designation, the libero no longer counts as LFP on the court in this team.

question: At what point before the game are the match balls distributed?

answers.a: The 2nd referee distributes two match balls to the ball retrievers after the presentation of the starting players of both teams and before checking the starting line-ups; he gives the third match ball to the serving player after checking the starting line-ups.
answers.b: The 2nd referee distributes two match balls to the ball retrievers and the third match ball to the serving player after the presentation of the starting players of both teams and before checking the starting line-ups.
answers.c: The 2nd referee distributes the three match balls to the ball retrievers after checking the starting line-ups.
answers.d: The 2nd referee distributes the three match balls to the ball retrievers after the presentation of the starting players of both teams and before checking the starting line-ups.
answers.e: The 2nd referee distributes the three match balls to the ball retrievers before the presentation of the starting players of the two teams.

question: During a substitution, it is determined (before the whistle for the next service) that the necessary number of LFP are no longer on the court after the substitution. What options does the team in question have and what sanctions must the referees apply?

answers.a: The team concerned has the right to cancel the substitution. For the resulting delay, the team in question shall receive a sanction for delay.
answers.b: The team concerned has the right to make another substitution during the same interruption (if the team is still entitled to do so) in order to comply with the regulations concerning LFP on the court. Because no effective delay occurs (a legal double substitution), no sanction for delay is imposed.
answers.c: The team concerned may continue to play with less LFP than necessary on the court. In this case, a fine will be imposed according to the catalog of fines. No sanction will be given for delay.
answers.d: The team concerned may continue to play with less LFP than necessary on the court. In this case, a fine shall be imposed in accordance with the catalog of fines. A sanction for delay shall be imposed on the team concerned.
answers.e: The team concerned has the right to make another substitution during the same interruption (if the team is still entitled to do so) in order to comply with the regulations concerning LFP on the court. For the resulting delay, the team concerned shall be sanctioned for delay.

question: The language can be selected in the eScoresheet application. Which language(s) should be selected for official Swiss Volley competitions?

answers.a: Native language of the scorer (if this is one of the four national languages of Switzerland)
answers.b: Native language of the 2nd referee (if this is one of the four national languages of Switzerland)
answers.c: German, French, Italian or English (the referees decide)
answers.d: English
answers.e: Native language of the 1st referee (if this is one of the four national languages of Switzerland)
answers.f: German, French, Italian or English (the scorer decides)

question: In the eScoresheet application 'Swiss Volley', the players of both teams are highlighted with specific colours. How many colours are used and which player categories are specially marked? (LFP: locally formed player; no colour is also considered a colour here)

answers.a: 2; LFP and non-LFP
answers.b: 4; 'normal' LFP, 'normal' non-LFP, LFP Libero(s) and non-LFP Libero(s)
answers.c: 3; 'normal' LFP, 'normal' non-LFP and Libero(s)
answers.d: 2; 'normal' players and libero(s)

question: In the eScoresheet application, the following seven options are offered for selection under Options - General. Which options should be selected?

answers.a: Libero in automatically (to be confirmed)
answers.b: Show a confirmation box (substitution and time-out)
answers.c: Manage captain on court
answers.d: Request to enter Libero at each start set
answers.e: Confirm when Libero out
answers.f: Check accidental clicking of the buttons (assign point, time-out)
answers.g: Propose starting line-up from prior set (when available)

question: In which area of the eScoresheet application must the number of spectators be entered (at the latest) at the end of the match?

answers.a: Comments
answers.b: Official Form
answers.c: Roster
answers.d: Options
answers.e: Match Info

question: The eScoresheet application has the two buttons 'Undo' and 'Replay'. Which statement(s) is (are) correct?

answers.a: Both buttons have the same consequences; in both actions, the last action previously performed is cancelled.
answers.b: In both actions, the last action previously performed is cancelled. When using 'Replay', however, an entry is made in the history of the match.
answers.c: The 'Undo' function should be used if the scorer has performed an incorrect action (an incorrect click).
answers.d: The 'Replay' function should be used if the referee changes his original decision.

question: At the end of the match, one of the two teams refuses to "sign" the eScoresheet, i.e. to confirm the result of the match by entering the password. What should the referees do in such a situation? (indicate all applicable answers)

answers.a: The eScoresheet of the match cannot be finalised. The laptop must not be switched off and must be sent to Swiss Volley (office) as soon as possible in this condition. Swiss Volley will then finalise the match.
answers.b: The eScoresheet of the match cannot be finalised. The eScoresheet must be printed out (if possible) and saved as a pdf-file. The printout and the pdf-file must be sent to Swiss Volley.
answers.c: After the match, Swiss Volley (office) and the Swiss Refereeing Commission (president) must be informed of the incident by means of a short e-mail.
answers.d: The team in question shall be sanctioned for delay.
answers.e: The eScoresheet must be confirmed by the referees with the master password on behalf of this team.
answers.f: Using the 'Comments' function, the referees must make a corresponding entry in the 'Scoresheet Notes' (or have it made by the scorer).
answers.g: The eScoresheet of the match cannot be finalised. The 1st referee must call the Swiss Volley hotline and follow their instructions.
answers.h: The team in question must be persuaded by the referees, if necessary under threat of sanctions, to confirm the result of the match by entering the password.

question: Which of these playing actions - concerning a team's first touch of the ball - are correct according to the rules?

answers.a: The ball bounces back from the opponent's block and hits the player's head and then his arms during the same action.
answers.b: During the service reception, a player plays the ball with an overhand finger pass and the ball touches one hand and the other in succession within one action.
answers.c: The ball was deflected by the player's own team's block and the player plays the ball with his foot.
answers.d: During the defence of the opponent's attack, a player plays the ball with an overhand finger pass and the ball touches one hand and the other in succession within one action.

question: When the 2nd referee checks the positions of the players on the court before the start of the set, he notices a discrepancy between the line-up sheet and the positions of the players. Which of the following statements are correct?

answers.a: The coach may request a substitution and sustitute the player on the line-up sheet with the player who is actually on the court.
answers.b: The players may correct their positions according to the line-up sheet and the team will be sanctioned with a warning for delay.
answers.c: The players must correct their positions according to the line-up sheet without being sanctioned.
answers.d: The scorer can correct the score sheet so that it matches the line-up of players on the court.

question: Team A blocks the opponent's attack in a regular manner, with their hands and the ball completely in the opponent's space. The ball bounces over the antenna into the free space above the free zone of team A. A player from Team A runs to play the ball back. Decide whether this is legal or not.

answers.a: Double fault, replay of the rally.
answers.b: Legal play, continue.
answers.c: Illegal play, ball out.

question: Team A blocks the opponent's attack in a regular manner, with the hands and the ball above their own half of the court. The ball bounces over the antenna into the free space above team B's free zone. A player from team A runs to return the ball. Decide whether this is legal or not.

answers.a: Double fault, replay of the rally.
answers.b: Illegal play, ball out.
answers.c: Legal play, continue.

question: When we talk about "influence" in the (wider) context of the net, which of the following statements is/are true?

answers.a: Interference is given when the player stands in the path of the opponent and thereby prevents the opponent from playing the ball.
answers.b: When entering the opponent's space, court and/or free zone, touching players automatically constitutes interference.
answers.c: Interference can be sanctioned, even if the contact is merely obstructive but not physical.
answers.d: If the contact with an opponent is accidental, this is still considered interference, even if it did not prevent the opponent from playing the ball.

question: During a match, a player from team B hit the ball against the opposing blocker (team A). The hands of the blocker were not over the court of B. The ball went from the hands of the blocker over the antenna, partially outside the crossing space and over the 1st referee into the free zone of B (see diagram). A second player from A ran after the ball to return it to his side of the net. The line judge signaled the ball as "out" and the 1st referee blew the whistle in B's favour. What should the 1st referee's interpretation of the rules be?

answers.a: The referee was right to blow the whistle.
answers.b: The referee should not have blown the whistle.
answers.c: The referee should decide on a replay.

question: Continuation of the previous question: The reason for this decision is:

answers.a: A player may not return a ball that comes from a block.
answers.b: The ball may be played back if it has completely or partially crossed the net plane outside the crossing space.
answers.c: The ball must not fly over the antenna into the free zone.

question: Which of the following statements regarding the competences and/or restrictions of a team's coach are correct?

answers.a: He may not leave his place on the bench closest to the scorer, but he may give instructions to the players on the court.
answers.b: He may ask the 1st or 2nd referee for explanations and clarifications of the rules.
answers.c: He may ask the scorer how many substitutions or time-outs he has already taken if this information is not shown on the electronic scoreboard.
answers.d: He may give instructions while standing or moving within the free zone in the area in front of his team bench without interfering with or delaying the game.

question: Which of the following actions are permitted?

answers.a: A player touches the opponent's half of the court with his hand (completely beyond the centre line) but does not interfere with the opponent's play.
answers.b: A player touches the opponent's half of the court with his foot (completely beyond the centre line) but does not interfere with the opponent's play.
answers.c: An attacker who lands after hitting the ball kicks the opposing blocker under the net, preventing the blocker from playing the ball.
answers.d: A player enters the opponent's space under the net with his body, but not his feet, but does not interfere with the opponent's play.

question: Which service action(s) are permitted?

answers.a: A server throws the ball up to serve, drops it on the floor, then throws the ball up again and touches the ball, all within eight seconds of the 1st referee's whistle to authorise the serve.
answers.b: A server throws the ball up eight seconds after the 1st referee's whistle to authorise the serve and then touches the ball half a second later.
answers.c: A server bounces the ball five times, then throws the ball up and touches it for the serve seven seconds after the 1st referee's whistle to authorise the serve.
answers.d: A server moves the ball backwards and forwards in his hands, then throws the ball up and touches the ball to serve six seconds after the 1st referee's whistle to authorise the serve.

question: Which of the following statements apply to regular ball play actions above the top of the net?

answers.a: Touching the ball in the opponent's space with a blocking action after the opponent's attacking hit.
answers.b: Touching the ball in the opponent's space at the same time as the opponent's attacking hit.
answers.c: Reaching over the net by the setter to touch the ball in the opponent's space and play it back to his side of the net.
answers.d: Simultaneous contact of the ball by opposing front-row players when the ball is directly over the top of the net.

question: At the moment of the 1st referee's whistle for the serve, only five players of the receiving team are on the court. The libero and the player he is replacing are standing in the warm-up area and make no attempt to enter the court. What is the referees' reaction if they only realise this during the rally?

answers.a: If the situation is recognised after the end of the rally, the result of that rally should be cancelled and the rally should be replayed without sanctions.
answers.b: Fault by the 1st referee. The rally is stopped immediately and replayed, without any sanctions.
answers.c: If the situation is only recognised after the whistle has been blown for the start of the next rally, the incorrect rally can no longer be replayed.
answers.d: The rally must be stopped immediately. It is replayed. The receiving team is sanctioned for the delay.
answers.e: The play continues. After the rally, the 1st referee issues a verbal warning to the captain of the receiving team.
answers.f: The rally continues and after the rally the receiving team is sanctioned for delay.

question: Continuation of the previous question: What is the correct reaction of the referees if they notice this before the 1st referee whistles for the serve?

answers.a: The serving team is awarded one point in the event of a warning for the receiving team.
answers.b: Issuing a warning for minor misconduct to the receiving team.
answers.c: Issuing a sanction for delay on the receiving team.
answers.d: The serving team is awarded one point in the event of a penalty for the receiving team.

question: After the second touch of the ball by one team, the ball crosses the plane of the net outside the antenna into the opposing team's free zone. Player #5 of this team runs after the ball to return it to his own half of the court. At what moment is a fault to be whistled?

answers.a: When player #5 touches the ball.
answers.b: If the ball flies out of the opponent's free zone (e.g. over the opponent's team bench).
answers.c: If the ball is touched by another player of the team after it has been correctly returned.
answers.d: Immediately when the ball crosses the plane of the net after the second touch of this team.

question: A blocker from team A wants to block the ball, which flies over the net in the direction of his playing space after the opponent's reception. Team B's setter passes the ball back to a team-mate, but the blocker touches the setter’s hand during the playing action (at the same moment). Which player makes a mistake?

answers.a: Double fault, rally is replayed.
answers.b: Fault by team B (setter) if the setter’s hand is in the opponent's playing space.
answers.c: Fault by team A (blocker) if the hand of the setter is in his own playing space.
answers.d: Fault by team A (blocker) in any case, the setter must never be disturbed when playing the ball.

question: An attacker hits the ball and, on landing, touching the ground with both feet, takes a small step and touches the net (inside the antennae), trying to regain balance. The ball is still in play. The player has not used the net as a stabilising aid. Which of the following statements is/are correct?

answers.a: This can never be a fault.
answers.b: The landing action ends when the player is able to start a new action as soon as he has regained his balance after landing (has landed safely).
answers.c: This is only a fault if the player has touched the ball.
answers.d: The landing action is always completed the moment the player touches the floor with both feet.
answers.e: If the referee still judges this movement of the player as "in the action of playing the ball ", he will whistle a net fault.

question: A player lands safely after a block, turns and pushes the net aside. He then stretches to play the ball as it falls to the ground, touching the net during this new action. Which of the following statements is/are correct?

answers.a: Touching the net when turning is not a fault.
answers.b: Only a blocking action at the net should be whistled as a fault.
answers.c: The first fault occurs when he turns.
answers.d: The first fault occurs when he stretches to play the ball in a new action and touches the net.

question: A player passes the ball to the middle of the net for a combination attack. An attacker and a blocker were in the middle position and another player some distance away in position 2. The ball went to the middle attacker. Indicate the correct statement(s):

answers.a: The player in position 2 commits a fault if he touches the net during this playing action.
answers.b: The setter commits a fault if he touches the net during this playing action.
answers.c: The attacker commits a fault if he touches the net during this playing action.
answers.d: The opposing block player commits a fault if he touches the net during this playing action.

question: In the middle of the 2nd set, a substitute player from team A appears in the substitution zone and thus requests a substitution. The scorer draws the attention of the referees to the fact that this player is not listed in the team list of the score sheet. During the subsequent closer check, it is also discovered that this player was already substituted in the 1st set at the score of 7:7 and substituted back again at the score of 9:9 (without the fault being noticed). How to proceed?

answers.a: The 1st set will be scored 25:0 for team B.
answers.b: The substitution is not allowed, the team receives a sanction for delay.
answers.c: The substitution will not be allowed, this will be considered as an improper request.
answers.d: The 1st set will be scored 25:7 for team B.
answers.e: The match will continue without further consequences as the 1st set is finished.

question: Player #3 from team A is substituted by player #8. One rally later, the libero replaces player #8 on the court. Shortly afterwards, when the score is 14:15 from team A's point of view, the libero leaves the court and player #3 returns on court again. The mistake goes unnoticed and team B wins the next rally. At this moment, team A realises the mistake, player #8 quickly runs onto the court and player #3 leaves. At what score does the game continue?

answers.a: 14:17 from team A's perspective.
answers.b: 14:15 from team A's perspective.
answers.c: 14:16 from team A's perspective.

question: The player to be substituted on court is waiting correctly in the substitution zone. The player on court who is to be substituted delays the substitution by walking very slowly towards the substitution zone. He has already been cautioned for minor misconduct by being shown a yellow card. What is the referee's reaction now?

answers.a: The substitution is rejected because the request is unauthorised.
answers.b: The substitution is rejected and the team is sanctioned for delay.
answers.c: The substitution is allowed, but the team is subsequently sanctioned for delay.
answers.d: The substitution is allowed, but the substituted player is subsequently penalised for rude conduct.

question: At the score of 14:14, Team A asks for the correct service player. The scorer explains that #3 is serving. Team A wins the next two points and Team B takes a time-out. After the time-out it becomes clear that #7, not #3, would have been the correct server. How should the game be continued?

answers.a: The game resumes at 14:14 with #7 serving for Team A. The time-out remains valid.
answers.b: The game resumes at 14:14 with #7 serving for Team A. The time-out is cancelled and is available to Team B again.
answers.c: Team B receives the service and one point. The game resumes at 16:15. The time-out remains valid.
answers.d: Team B receives the service and one point. The game resumes at 14:15. The time-out is cancelled and is available to Team B again.

question: Which of the following attack actions are faulty?

answers.a: The libero executes an attack hit after jumping in the front zone, with the ball partially below the top of the net.
answers.b: The libero executes an attack hit after juming in the front zone, with the ball partially above the top of the net.
answers.c: A back-row player executes an attack hit after jumping in the front zone, whereby the ball is completely above the top of the net at the moment of hitting the ball.
answers.d: A player executes an attack hit directly after the opposing team's serve, with the ball in the back zone completely above the top of the net.

question: After a poor reception, the ball is heading under the net (at a height of about one meter) towards the opponent's side of the court. The server wants to play the ball back into his own half of the court. In which cases is this allowed?

answers.a: Only if the player touches the ball completely in his own half of the court.
answers.b: Always, as long as the ball has not completely crossed the lower space of the net.
answers.c: Only if the ball is still completely in their own half of the court.
answers.d: Always, as long as the ball has only half crossed the lower space of the net.

question: The team captain #1 is not on the court. The game captain #5 is substituted by #7, #9 becomes the new game captain. After one rally, #5 returns to the court. Who is now game captain?

answers.a: Player #1 because he is the team captain and the game captain is not clearly defined.
answers.b: Player #5 because he was named game captain first.
answers.c: Player #9 because he was last named as game captain.
answers.d: The game captain must be redefined because he is not clearly defined.

question: Which of the following actions during the game must the 2nd referee whistle for within his area of responsibility?

answers.a: Attack hit fault by a back-row player.
answers.b: Position faults by the receiving team.
answers.c: Block attempt by a back-row player (not the libero).
answers.d: Executed block by a back-row player.

question: The assistant coach of team A hands the line-up sheet to the 2nd referee shortly before the start of the next set. When checking the positions on the court, the 2nd referee notices that player #5 is on the court instead of player #8, who is on the line-up sheet. He informs the coach that player #5 is not allowed on the court without a legal substitution. The coach replies that he can still change the starting line-up because his signature is missing on the line-up sheet. What do the referees have to do?

answers.a: Since the 2nd referee did not realise that the line-up sheet was not signed, the coach has the right to correct the line-up sheet. Since the 2nd referee made a mistake, team A cannot be sanctioned for delay.
answers.b: Even if the signature is missing on the line-up sheet, it remains valid. Therefore, player #8 must enter the cort or a legal substitution must be requested.
answers.c: Since the 2nd referee did not realise that the line-up sheet was not signed, the coach has the right to correct the line-up sheet. Team A is sanctioned for delay.
answers.d: The 2nd referee makes sure that the coach of team A signs the line-up sheet before the start of the next set.

question: After a bad service reception, the ball crosses the vertical plane of the net completely outside the crossing space in the direction of the opponent's free zone. A player runs after the ball and passes the ball back to his team completely inside the crossing space. What signals the line judge in charge?

answers.a: The line judge raises the flag vertically at the moment the player touches the ball in the opponent's free zone.
answers.b: The line judge raises the flag vertically at the moment when the ball crosses the vertical plane of the net completely within the crossing space in the direction of the respective court.
answers.c: The line jduge waves the flag and points to the antenna at the moment when the ball crosses the vertical plane of the net completely within the crossing space in the direction of the respective court or when it is completely above the opponent's court.
answers.d: The line judge does not signal anything because there is no fault.

question: What conditions must be met for the 1st referee to authorise the serve?

answers.a: The service player is in possession of the ball and has eye contact with the 1st referee.
answers.b: The service player is in possession of the ball and both teams are ready to play.
answers.c: The service player is in the service zone and both teams are ready to play.
answers.d: The service player is in the service zone and in possession of the ball.

question: Team A has already made five substitutions in the current 2nd set when a player is injured and cannot continue playing. This player has already been substituted back once in the current set. How should the match be continued?

answers.a: Team A has the right to make an exceptional substitution. Any player who is not on the court at the moment of the injury – with the exception of the libero, the second libero or their regular replacement player - may be substituted. Team A is then entitled to one more legal substitution.
answers.b: A player cannot be substituted twice during a set. Team A is therefore declared incomplete for the current set and loses this set.
answers.c: Team A has the right to make an exceptional substitution. Any player who is not on the court at the moment of the injury – with the exception of the libero, the second libero or their regular replacement player – may be sustituted. This means that the maximum number of six substitutions per set has been reached and only exceptional substitutions are permitted in the current set.
answers.d: A player cannot be substituted twice during a set. Team A is therefore declared incomplete and loses the match.

question: A team plays with two liberos (L1 and L2). Libero L1 is disqualified, then libero L2 is injured. The team coach re-designates a new libero L3. Which statements are correct?

answers.a: The team may not play with two liberos for the rest of the match.
answers.b: The team can re-designate another player as second libero L4.
answers.c: If the team captain is to be re-designated as libero L3, he must first surrender all his rights in this regard.
answers.d: When libero L2 has recovered from the injury, he can act as a second libero alongside libero L3.

question: Team A plays with only one libero. The libero gets injured. The coach decides to re-designate a new libero. Which of the statements are correct?

answers.a: The injured libero may not re-enter the match, but he may sit on the bench during the match.
answers.b: The coach must re-designate a player as the new libero who was not on the court at the moment of the injury.
answers.c: The player re-designated as libero must retain the function as libero for the remainder of the match (except when the libero is again re-designated).
answers.d: The coach must re-designate a player as the new libero who has not played in that set until the moment of the injury.

question: Player #8 is registered as team captain. Player #12 is also registered as coach. The coach is substituted on court and immediately afterwards requests a time-out. There was no improper request or delay in the match up to this point. In the 1st set, however, the coach had already been warned for minor misconduct by being shown a yellow card. What is the correct reaction of the referees now?

answers.a: The request is rejected, the first time without a sanction for delay, the second and each subsequent time with a sanction for delay.
answers.b: The request is rejected and the team is sanctioned for delay.
answers.c: The request is rejected and the coach is penalised for rude conduct.
answers.d: The request is granted and the time-out is given.

question: A rally is won by the receiving team A. At the beginning of the rally, the scorer was unable to recognise the number of the serving player. Only after the rally has ended does the scorer realise that the wrong player has served the ball and immediately informs the 2nd referee. How do the referees decide correctly?

answers.a: Team A is awarded two points; one because of team B's rotational fault and one because the rally was completed and team A won it.
answers.b: Team A is awarded a point due to team B's rotational fault. The rally itself is cancelled.
answers.c: The rally is replayed. The score does not change because the referees have recognised the rotation fault too late.
answers.d: Team A is awarded a point due to winning the rally. As a result of the rotational fault, the rally is replayed with the correct server from team B.

question: A player shows misbehaviour for the first time. In which cases is an expulsion the sanction provided for in the volleyball rules?

answers.a: After several faulty receptions, the setter shouts "idiot" at one of his team's two receiving players and shows him a 'bird'.
answers.b: The player tries to hit an opponent but does not touch him.
answers.c: After a net decision, the player shows the 2nd referee the 'middle finger'.
answers.d: The player threatens a line judge with his fist.

question: The entries in the score sheet (e-scoresheet) have been confirmed by the team captain and the coach before the match. In the 2nd set, coach A wants to replace player #7 of the starting lineup with player #15. A #15 is not recorded in the score sheet. The scorer reports this to the 2nd referee after player #15 has entered the substitution zone. Which of the statements are correct?

answers.a: If the player is registered in the score sheet with a different number, the 1st referee allows the substitution and in the score sheet the number of the player is corrected. Team A is sanctioned for delay.
answers.b: If the player is not recorded in the score sheet at all, the 1st referee does not approve the substitution. The coach A is given a warning.
answers.c: If the player is not recorded in the score sheet at all, the 1st referee does not approve the substitution. Team A is sanctioned for delay.
answers.d: If the player is registered in the score sheet with a different number, the 1st referee allows the substition and in the score sheet the number of the player is corrected without sanction.

question: The libero of team A acrobatically defends a short tip by the attacker of team B and is able to keep the ball in play. After touching the ball, the libero slides under the net while lying on the ground; in the end, his entire body is completely on Team B's side of the court. The libero then immediately moves back into his own side of the court. During the entire action, his two feet never touch the opponent's court. He also does not interfere with the players of team B. How do the referees decide correctly?

answers.a: The referees do not interrupt the rally. There is no fault because the libero neither touches the opponent's half of the court with his feet nor interferes with the opponent's players when playing the ball.
answers.b: The referees interrupt the rally. Because the libero ends up lying completely in the opponent's half of the court and no part of his body is in contact with his own half of the court, a fault has been committed.

question: Team A serves. Team B receives with the setter in position 6. When releasing from the net, the diagonal player of team B takes a step and stands with one foot on the centre line at the moment of the serve. In doing so, he also touches the opponent's court, but without interfering with the players of team A. What is the correct decision by the referees?

answers.a: The referees do not stop the rally. Team B has made a mistake, but because Team A's players are not hindered in their actions, this should not be sanctioned ("keep the ball flying").
answers.b: The referees stop the rally and decide that team B has committed a fault because a player of team B (the diagonal player) touches the opponent's court at the moment of the serve.
answers.c: The referees do not stop the rally because there is no fault.

question: Team A wants to make a double substitution. One player enters the substitution zone ready to play, while the second player is still looking for the correct substitution paddle. Shortly before the first substitution is completed, the second player also enters the substitution zone ready to play. How should the referees react?

answers.a: The 2nd referee authorises both substitutions. As the second substitution player was ready before the first substitution was completed, there was effectively no delay. In such cases, the referees should interpret the rules generously.
answers.b: The 2nd referee rejects both substitutions because the two substitution players did not enter the substitution zone at the same time. The 1st referee sanctions team A for delay.
answers.c: The 2nd referee only authorises the first substitution; the second substitution is rejected because the second substitute was not ready to play at the moment of the request. The 1st referee sanctions team A for delay.
answers.d: The 2nd referee only authorises the first substitution; the second substitution is rejected without consequences because the second substitute was not ready to play at the moment of the request.

question: Team B requests a substitution. This is rejected by the 2nd referee because the substitute player entered the substitution zone with the wrong substitution paddle. Because team B had already received a delay warning, they are now penalised by the 1st referee for delay. As a result, team B requests another substitution before the match is resumed. How should the referees react?

answers.a: The referees reject the second request for a substitution. The 2nd referee has an improper request noted on the score sheet.
answers.b: The referees reject the second request for a substitution. The first referee again penalises the team for delay.
answers.c: The referees allow this substitution.
answers.d: The referees reject the second request for a substitution without any further consequences.

question: Team A requests a time-out after a request for a substitution has already been denied in the same interruption and the team has been sanctioned with a delay warning. How should the referees react?

answers.a: The referees grant the request for time-out.
answers.b: The referees reject the request for time-out. The 2nd referee has an improper request recorded on the score sheet.
answers.c: The referees reject the request for time-out without further consequences.
answers.d: The referees reject the request for time-out. The 1st referee awards a delay penalty.

question: How should the 1st referee react if a game captain asks to check the positions of the opposing team's players?

answers.a: To provide answers and detailed information on the positions.
answers.b: The 1st referee should not answer. The game captain can only ask to check the positions of his own team.
answers.c: First answer only with hand signals. Follow-up questions can then be answered verbally.
answers.d: To Answer and indicate whether the positions of the opposing team are correct or not.

question: The team captain is injured before the toss. What is the correct procedure for nominating a new team captain?

answers.a: The coach can nominate another player as the new team captain. A strip of tape is stuck under the number on the front of the jersey and the number on the score sheet is circled.
answers.b: It is too late to appoint a new team captain. The coach may appoint a "general game captain" who fulfils the duties of the team captain and who signs the score sheet after the match.
answers.c: The coach can nominate two other players to act as co-captains. Both players are marked on the score sheet by circling their names.
answers.d: It is too late to nominate a new team captain. The coach fulfils the duties of the team captain during the match and signs the score sheet instead of the team captain.

question: A player thinks she has noticed a wet spot on the floor. She calls the assistant coach to the side line near the team bench to help find the wet spot, preventing the 1st referee from immediately blowing the whistle to continue the match. What should the 1st referee do?

answers.a: He should inform the assistant coach via the game captain to return to the team bench.
answers.b: He should leave the team their way and do nothing.
answers.c: He should personally inform the assistant coach to go back to the team bench.
answers.d: He should instruct the 2nd referee to tell the game captain to inform the assistant coach to go back to the team bench.
answers.e: He should issue a sanction to the team for delay.

question: After hitting the serve, the scorer reports an incorrect service player of team A. What happens next?

answers.a: All points that were clearly scored with the wrong service player are cancelled. Team A corrects the line-up and then rotates. Team B receives a point and the right to serve and does not rotate.
answers.b: All points that were clearly scored with the wrong service player are cancelled. Team A corrects the line-up and retains the right to serve. Team B does not rotate.
answers.c: All points that were clearly scored with the wrong service player are cancelled. Team A rotates. Team B receives a point and the right to serve and rotates by one position.
answers.d: All points that were clearly scored with the wrong service player are cancelled. Team A corrects the line-up. Team B receives a point and the right to serve and rotates by one position.

question: Player A is on the line-up sheet, but is injured immediately before the match. What happens next?

answers.a: The substitution of player A takes place on the court at the start of the match. Before the first serve, the game captain must request a time-out and the coach must report the substitution directly to the scorer, who then records everything on the score sheet.
answers.b: Player A's team starts the match with only five players on the court. A substitution can only be requested by the coach after at least one rally has been completed. The substitution is then carried out in a regular way.
answers.c: Player A may be replaced by any player not listed on the line-up sheet. The coach can have the scorer make the substitution on the score sheet before the first serve. Informing the 2nd referee is optional.
answers.d: The situation is treated as a legal substitution. After the 2nd referee has checked the line-up sheet, the coach requests the substitution by hand signal to the 2nd referee, who also shows the hand signal for substitution. The substitution is registered on the score sheet.

question: Before the third set, the 2nd referee whistles the teams back onto the court. One team does not react. This team has not yet received a sanction for delay. How should the referees react?

answers.a: The 1st referee issues a "sanction for delay" (warning). If the team still does not react, the 1st referee must impose a further sanction for delay (penalty).
answers.b: The 2nd referee goes to the team to remind them to return to the court. If the team does not respond, the 2nd referee will impose a sanction for delay (yellow card, covering the wrist).
answers.c: Whistle again and then once more. If the team does not react to the third whistle, the 1st referee must issue a penalty for delay (red card, covering the wrist).
answers.d: Issue a penalty for delay (red card, covering the wrist).

question: The libero was on the court for player A and was expelled for the remainder of this set. There are three ways in which the game can be continued. Which of the following suggestions is NOT a way to continue the game?

answers.a: If the team has only one libero, the team can immediately re-designate a new libero from the players who are not currently on the court (except for the libero's replacement player). The acting/expelled libero is not allowed to play for the remainder of the match.
answers.b: If the team has two liberos, the second libero can be exchanged immediately for the acting/expelled libero.
answers.c: If the team has two liberos, the team may decide to expell the second libero for the remainder of the match, allowing the acting libero to continue playing in this set.
answers.d: If the team has only one libero, player A can return onto the court. The team can continue to play without a libero.

question: Player A runs into the stands adjacent to his side of the court to play the ball. Just before contacting the ball, a spectator knocks the ball away. What is the correct action of the referee and why?

answers.a: Whistle. Fault. These are external influences.
answers.b: Whistle. Fault. A player may never enter the spectator stands.
answers.c: Whistle and hand signal "out" (hand signal #15). The players have no priority when playing the ball outside the playing area.
answers.d: Whistle. Fault. The player may not leave the free zone to play a ball in the spectator stands.

question: Player A, a setter, reaches over the top of the net to the opponent's side and passes the ball to a team-mate to attack. What should the referee decide?

answers.a: Nothing. Playing the ball in this way is in accordance with the rules, as player A is trying to pass and not to attack himself.
answers.b: This is a fault in any case, as players are not allowed to reach into the opponent's playing space (even if the ball is not touched).
answers.c: This is a fault because a player must touch the ball on his own side above the top of the net (except when blocking).
answers.d: Nothing. Playing the ball in this way is in accordance with the rules, as there is no contact with the opposing team.

question: Player A, a middle blocker, jumps up at the net to block and touches the top of the net when attempting to block. There is no contact with the ball. What should happen and why?

answers.a: This is not a net fault, as a blocking player is allowed to touch the net during play.
answers.b: This is a net fault by player A, as players never have the right to touch the top of the net.
answers.c: This is a net fault by player A (touching the net during an action in which he tries to play the ball).
answers.d: This is not a net fault because player A does not touch the ball.

question: A player from the starting line-up has been substituted and later returns to the court. Shortly afterwards, he injures himself and is no longer able to continue playing. Who can be substituted for this player?

answers.a: Any player who is not on the court, except the libero or his replacement player.
answers.b: No player. The team must continue to play with only five players.
answers.c: The libero or the player who is currently replaced by the acting libero.
answers.d: Any player who is not currently on the court.

question: Team A substitutes player A for player B. At the same time, the coach indicates that player C should replace the libero. What should the referees decide?

answers.a: Allow either the replacement or the substitution. The other action can be completed at the end of the next rally.
answers.b: Either allow the substitution or the replacement and issue a sanction for delay against team A. The other action can be completed at the end of the next rally.
answers.c: If a substitution or a replacement occurs at the same time, the only player who can be replaced for the libero is the freshly substituted player. The referee should allow player B to replace the libero.
answers.d: Allow both: substitution and replacement.

question: Team A forgets to replace the libero when he rotates to position IV. After three points, the referee realises that the libero is illegally on the court. Which of the following are the correct consequences of this situation?

answers.a: The points scored by team A with the wrong line-up are cancelled.
answers.b: The line-up must be corrected.
answers.c: Point and service to the opponent.
answers.d: Expulsion of the libero.

question: Between two sets, the 1st referee issues a red card to a player from team A for rude conduct. Team A will have the first serve in the next set. What happens now?

answers.a: Team A starts the set at 0:2 and retains the right to serve.
answers.b: Team A starts the set at 0:0 and retains the right to serve.
answers.c: Team A starts the set at 0:1 and retains the right to serve.
answers.d: Team A starts the set at 0:1 and loses the right to serve.

question: A team plays with two liberos. One libero is expelled. A few rallies later, the other libero is declared unable to play. Which statement(s) is (are) NOT correct?

answers.a: The libero who is unable to play may be replaced by a re-designated libero. Only players who have not yet taken part in a substitution may be re-designated as libero.
answers.b: The coach immediately re-designates the replacement player of the libero who is unable to play as the new libero.
answers.c: The coach may not re-designate another player as libero, as the player who has been expelled may play again in the next set. A re-designation would be permitted in the 5th set.

question: Player #8 is formally warned (yellow card) in the 2nd set for minor misconduct. In the 4th set, player #2 of the same team shows a similar behaviour. What is the procedure?

answers.a: Player #2 receives a yellow card for minor misconduct.
answers.b: Player #2 is given a verbal warning because he has not yet received a yellow card, but a team cannot receive two yellow cards during one match.
answers.c: Player #2 is penalised (red card) for repeated minor misconduct by this team.

question: A ball flies below the bottom of the net between the post and the extension of the antenna towards the opponent's court. Which statement(s) is (are) correct?

answers.a: A player may enter the opponent's space to return the ball that has completely crossed the lower space of the net as long as he can reach the ball without stepping over the centre line.
answers.b: If the ball has completely crossed the lower space under the net, the ball is "out" (hand signal #15).
answers.c: If the ball has completely crossed the lower space under the net, hand signal #22 ("ball crossing the lower space" under the net) must be shown.

question: A player from team A attacks a ball that is directly above the top of the net. At the same time, a blocker from team B touches the ball without reaching over the net. After the simultaneous contact, the ball falls into the free zone of team B. How should the 1st referee decide?

answers.a: Team B wins the rally, as the fault is attributed to team A (ball falls into team B's free zone).
answers.b: A blocking player may not touch the ball before or at the same time as the attacking hit, therefore team A wins the rally.
answers.c: Replay (double fault) because the ball was caught above the top of the net.

question: A ball is played by a player of Team A and then touches a coaching staff member of Team A. Which hand signal is to be shown?

answers.a: Hand signal "ball touched" (hand signal #24).
answers.b: Hand signal ball "out" (hand signal #15).

question: Continuation of the previous question: Which hand signal should be used when the ball touches a coaching staff member of the opposing Team B?

answers.a: Hand signal ball "out" (hand signal #15).
answers.b: Hand signal "ball touched" (hand signal #24).

question: Which referee blows the whistle to send the teams onto the court before the start of a set in an NLA match?

answers.a: In any case, the 1st referee.
answers.b: In any case, the 2nd referee.
answers.c: Before the 1st and 5th set the 1st referee, otherwise the 2nd referee.
answers.d: Before the 1st set none of the referees, the speaker calls the players onto the court; before the 2nd to 5th set the 2nd referee.

question: In the 3rd set, with the score 17:9 and team A serving, player #11 from team A enters the substitution zone. Player #3, who had been on the court since the beginning of the set, comes to the sideline. At that moment, the scorer realises that player #3 is not on the team list. It is also noted that player #3 was substituted at 19:11 in the 1st set and was substituted again at 24:19. This set was won by team A with 25:21. There was also issued a delay warning against team A. The 2nd set was won by team A without player #3 being in play. How should the refereeing corps deal with this situation?

answers.a: The 1st set is re-assessed: Team B wins it 25:19 and the result of the 2nd set remains unchanged. In the current 3rd set, the points scored by team A up to that moment are cancelled. In addition, team A is sanctioned for delay. Score in the 3rd set: 10:0 in favour of team B. Serve for team B.
answers.b: As team A used a player who was not listed in the team list, they lose the match 0:3 (0:75 ball points).
answers.c: The 1st set is re-assessed: Team B wins it 25:19 and the result of the 2nd set remains unchanged. In the current 3rd set, team B is awarded a point and the right to serve; the points scored by team A in the 3rd set up to that moment are cancelled. Score in the 3rd set: 10:0 in favour of team B.
answers.d: The 1st set is re-assessed: Team B wins it 25:0 and the result of the 2nd set remains unchanged. In the current 3rd set, the points scored by team A up to that moment are cancelled. Score in the 3rd set: 9:0 in favour of team B. Serve for team B.

question: During a rally in the 1st set, a player from team A is seriously injured. Which statement(s) is (are) correct?

answers.a: An injured player must be substituted exceptionally. If this is the first substitution made by team A, the team can make a further six substitutions.
answers.b: The referee stops the rally and the rally is then replayed.
answers.c: Medical treatment inside the playing area must be completed after 3 minutes at the latest. After that, treatment continues outside the playing area.
answers.d: A player who has been substituted exceptionally may no longer take part in the match.

question: In the 3rd set of a match, the score is 8:8. After the following rally, two opposing players have an altercation under the net, prompting the 1st referee to penalise both players for rude conduct. How should the sanction be issued according to the rules?

answers.a: The 1st referee calls both game captains and first imposes the penalty on the player of the team not serving next.
answers.b: The 1st referee calls both players to be penalised and first imposes the penalty to the player of the team serving next.
answers.c: The 1st referee calls both game captains and first imposes the penalty to the player of the team serving next.
answers.d: The 1st referee shows both players the red card at the same time – without directly addressing the game captains or the two players.
answers.e: The 1st referee calls both players to be penalised and first imposes the penalty on the player of the team not serving next.

question: Overhand finger passes are a technique judged by the 1st referee. Which of the following statements are correct?

answers.a: The 1st referee automatically assesses a spinning ball as faulty.
answers.b: The 1st referee stops (whistles) long or extended contacts with the ball.
answers.c: The 1st referee only whistles if a clear double touch was visible.
answers.d: The 1st referee does not judge a ball on the basis of its spin.

question: After the end of a rally, the captain comes to the 1st referee to ask him about the ball handling during the game. The 1st referee's answer should initially be:

answers.a: After a brief explanation, a yellow card is shown for minor misconduct.
answers.b: After a brief explanation, issue a verbal warning.
answers.c: Wave the player off and penalise him with a red card.
answers.d: After a brief explanation, ask the player to resume play, as ball handling matters are entirely at the judgement of the 1st referee.

question: Continuation of the previous question: After the captain, another player came to question the 1st referee. What answer should the 1st referee give?

answers.a: Ask the player to resume play, as ball handling matters are entirely at the judgement of the 1st referee.
answers.b: Issue a warning to the player, verbally or with a yellow card.
answers.c: Issuing a delay sanction.
answers.d: Waving the player off and refusing to listen.

question: Teams have the right to request multiple consecutive regular game interruptions during an interruption, but not in all cases. Please answer which combinations may be granted DURING THE SAME INTERRUPTION according to the rules: Explanation: S: Substitution / T: Time-out / A: Team A / B: Team B

answers.a: SA-SB-TA-TB-SA-TA
answers.b: SA-SB-TA-TB-TA-TB
answers.c: SB-TB-SA-SB
answers.d: SA-TB-SB-TA-TB-TA
answers.e: TA-SA-TB-TA-SB

question: Indicate each individual step that is not allowed by ticking each corresponding box that makes the sequence illegal. Take a look at this sequence:

answers.a: Libero enters the court.
answers.b: Rally.
answers.c: Libero leaves the court.
