answers.d: During the next rally, the 1st referee whistles a double fault (to replay the rally)
answers.e: Libero re-enters the court.
answers.f: The 1st referee whistles for the serve of the repeated rally.

question: Continuation of the previous question: What is wrong with the sequence? I.e. why did you choose this/these point/s of the sequence?

answers.a: There must be a completed rally between two libero replacements.
answers.b: The 1st referee is not allowed to whistle for a double fault.
answers.c: This is an illegal libero replacement.
answers.d: The libero may only re-enter the court after a completed rally.
answers.e: The libero must be substituted like any other player.
answers.f: The libero needs the permission of the 2nd referee to leave the court.
answers.g: The libero needs the permission of the 2nd referee to enter the court.
answers.h: The libero may only enter the court at the start of a set.
answers.i: Replay of the rally does not exist in Volleyball.
answers.j: Only the 2nd referee may whistle for a double fault.

question: The game captain of team A demanded a time-out by showing the respective hand signal. Immediately afterwards, the coach of team A withdrew this time-out by shouting "No." to the 2nd referee. What is the correct decision now?

answers.a: Time-out may not be granted.
answers.b: Time-out must be granted.

question: Continuation of the previous question: Reason for this decision:

answers.a: Only the team captain may request time-outs and player substitutions in the absence of the coach.
answers.b: The coach is the first in the team's command structure – the captain may only request time-outs and player substitutions in the absence of the coach.
answers.c: Only the game captain may request time-outs and player substitutions.

question: Continuation of the previous question: Indicate the additional actions of the 1st referee, if any:

answers.a: This is in any case an improper request.
answers.b: If there is a delay, the 1st referee must issue a delay sanction.
answers.c: If there is no delay, the 1st referee should simply continue the game.

question: At the moment of the substitution request, the substitute player was still wearing his tracksuit jacket. Should the 2nd referee authorise the substitution?

answers.a: Yes.
answers.b: No.

question: After a substitution was requested by the substitute player entering the substitution zone, the scorer and the 2nd referee noticed that the player was wearing the wrong substitution paddle no. 7. The coach "changed his mind" at that moment and instructed the substitute to take paddle no. 9 instead. Which player must be substituted?

answers.a: No. 9.
answers.b: No. 7.
answers.c: No player.

question: The service player of team A was tipping the ball outside the service zone, enters the service zone and then serves; however, the ball was outside the service zone during the whole process (even at the moment of the serve). The 1st referee should ...

answers.a: ... allow the serve.
answers.b: ... call a fault.
answers.c: ... have the serve executed again.

question: Continuation of the previous question: The reason for this decision is ...

answers.a: ... that the ball must be inside the service zone at the moment of the hit.
answers.b: ... that only the position of the feet is relevant.
answers.c: ... that the player has started the movement outside the service zone.

question: A ball served by Team A goes toward the opponent, but catches on the top of the net and then falls down on A's side of the net. A player from Team B reaches under the net and catches the ball before it touches the ground. The 1st referee should ...

answers.a: ... whistle a double fault and replay the rally.
answers.b: ... whistle a fault of the serving player.
answers.c: ... whistle a fault of the player of Team B.

question: Continuation of the previous question: The reason for this decision is, ...

answers.a: ... that the service was not executed.
answers.b: ... that the ball failed to cross the vertical plane of the net through the crossing space.
answers.c: ... that the ball was still in play.

question: A player ran up the stands after the ball on his side of the court and, with the second hit, played the ball back into his own court from a position above the court. The 1st referee should ...

answers.a: ... do nothing.
answers.b: ... call a fault.
answers.c: ... call for a caught ball.

question: Continuation of the previous question: The reason for this decision is ...

answers.a: ... that the player may play the ball outside his own free zone.
answers.b: ... that the player may only play the ball within the playing area.
answers.c: ... that the player used assistance to play the ball.

question: In Switzerland, in official competitions, a team consists of a maximum of ...

answers.a: ... 14 players, one coach, two assistant coaches, one physiotherapist and one doctor.
answers.b: ... 14 players, one coach, one assistant coach, one athletic trainer, one physiotherapist and one doctor.
answers.c: ... 12 players, one coach, two assistant coaches, one physiotherapist and one doctor.
answers.d: ... 12 players, one coach, one assistant coach, one physiotherapist and one doctor.

question: In which of the following situations is there a mistake that leads to a point for the receiving team?

answers.a: Team A's service player hits the ball with both hands while serving when team B commits a positional fault.
answers.b: Team A's service player hits the ball while serving, standing on the baseline, and team B commits a positional fault.
answers.c: Team B commits a positional fault, whereupon team A's serve flies into the net.
answers.d: Team A's service player commits a rotational fault and team B commits a positional fault.

question: A player attacks and after hitting the ball touches the net meshes while landing.

answers.a: Touching the net is a fault and should primarily be whistled by the 1st referee.
answers.b: Nevertheless, touching the net is not a fault, as only contact with the upper top band of the net constitutes a fault.
answers.c: Touching the net is a fault that should primarily be whistled by the 2nd referee because it involves the lower part of the net.
answers.d: Touching the net is not a fault, as it is only a fault during the hitting movement.

question: After having whistled a net fault, the 2nd referee shows...

answers.a: ... the hand signal No. 19 "net contact by a player" and after the 1st referee the team serving next.
answers.b: ... only the hand signal No. 19 "net contact by a player" and the player.
answers.c: ... the hand signal No. 19 "net contact by a player", the player and then immediately the team serving next.
answers.d: ... the hand signal No. 19 "net contact by a player", the player (if necessary) and, after the 1st referee, the team serving next.

question: Indicate the correct statement(s):

answers.a: A player may use the net between the antennas as a support or stabilising aid.
answers.b: The line judge is responsible for indicating when a player touches the lower part of the antenna.
answers.c: A player may touch the net meshes outside the antenna as long as he does not interferes with play.
answers.d: A player interferes with the game by touching the antenna during an action of playing the ball.

question: During the serve, the player in position 6 wipes his hand briefly over a wet spot on the floor. His foot position is 'correct' in relation to the player in position 3 (i.e. he is standing behind him), but his hand is closer to the centre line than the feet of the player in position 3. Is this a fault?

answers.a: No.
answers.b: Yes, in any case.
answers.c: Yes, if the hand actually touches the ground at the moment of the service hit.

question: A player from team A is injured and the referee stops the rally immediately. While a doctor attends to the injured player on the court, a player from team B is disqualified. Which of the following statements are true?

answers.a: Team B is declared incomplete in any case.
answers.b: If team B can make a legal substitution for the disqualified player, the referee will allow this.
answers.c: If team B cannot substitute the disqualified player legally, the team is declared incomplete.

question: Team "A" plays with one libero. This libero is injured during a playing action. The rally has to be repeated. The coach decides to re-designate a new libero immediately. The injured libero leaves the court while the newly re-designated libero takes up his position on the court at the same time. Is this allowed?

answers.a: No, the coach is not allowed to re-designate a new libero during the match.
answers.b: No, a new rally must be made before the libero can be replaced.
answers.c: Yes.

question: The libero of team "A" is injured. The coach decides to re-designate a new libero. Which of the following statements is/are correct?

answers.a: The coach must re-designate a player as the new libero who was not on the court at the moment of the injury.
answers.b: The injured libero may not re-enter the match, but he may sit on the bench or be in the warm-up area during the match.
answers.c: The player re-designated as the new libero must retain the function as libero for the remainder of the match (except when the libero is again re-designated).

question: Is an injured player who has been substituted exceptionally allowed to sit on the bench during the match?

answers.a: Yes, but only with the permission of the 1st referee.
answers.b: No, he's not allowed to sit on the bench.
answers.c: Yes, he can sit on the bench.

question: The libero of team "A" suddenly felt dizzy during the 2nd set, had a severe headache and felt very ill. During the change of courts, the team doctor diagnosed him with high temperature (40°). Is the coach allowed to re-designate a new libero in this case?

answers.a: No, the coach can only re-designate a new libero in the event of an injury (with the prior authorisation of the 1st referee).
answers.b: The decision is at the discretion of the 1st referee.
answers.c: Yes.

question: During a rally, the ball touches the antenna on the 2nd referee's side. What is the correct decision of the 2nd referee?

answers.a: The 2nd referee does not indicate anything.
answers.b: The 2nd referee whistles and indicates the fault (out).
answers.c: The 2nd referee takes a step to the side of the team that committed the fault, makes the hand signal for out, but without whistling, to help the 1st referee.

question: Indicate whether the situations described below are against the rules. If so, which referee is responsible for blowing the whistle in this situation? 1. Successful attack hit of the front-row player after an overhand finger pass of the libero within the front zone. At the moment of the attack hit, the ball was above the top of the net with its entire circumference and the attacking player jumped within the back zone. The 1st referee could clearly see this game situation.

answers.a: Fault; 1st and 2nd referee.
answers.b: Fault; 1st referee.
answers.c: No fault.
answers.d: Fault; 2nd referee.

question: Indicate whether the situations described below are against the rules. If so, which referee is responsible for blowing the whistle in this situation? 2. Attempted block of a back-row player.

answers.a: Fault; 1st referee.
answers.b: Fault; 1st and 2nd referee.
answers.c: No fault.
answers.d: Fault; 2nd referee.

question: Indicate whether the situations described below are against the rules. If so, which referee is responsible for blowing the whistle in this situation? 3. After the service, the ball crosses the vertical plane of the net on the 2nd referee's side outside the crossing space.

answers.a: Fault; 1st and 2nd referee.
answers.b: Fault; 1st referee.
answers.c: No fault.
answers.d: Fault; 2nd referee.

question: Indicate whether the situations described below are against the rules. If so, which referee is responsible for blowing the whistle in this situation? 4. A player touches the net outside the antenna on the 1st referee's side during an action of playing the ball.

answers.a: Fault; 1st referee.
answers.b: No fault.
answers.c: Fault; 1st and 2nd referee.
answers.d: Fault; 2nd referee.

question: Indicate whether the situations described below are against the rules. If so, which referee is responsible for blowing the whistle in this situation? 5. Executed attack hit of the libero (played with overhand fingers) from the back zone, if the ball was above the top of the net with its full circumference at the moment of contact.

answers.a: Fault; 1st referee.
answers.b: No fault.
answers.c: Fault; 1st and 2nd referee.
answers.d: Fault; 2nd referee.

question: Indicate whether the situations described below are against the rules. If so, which referee is responsible for blowing the whistle in this situation? 6. The ball touches the ceiling of the sport hall.

answers.a: Fault; 1st referee.
answers.b: No fault.
answers.c: Fault; 1st and 2nd referee.
answers.d: Fault; 2nd referee.

question: Indicate whether the situations described below are against the rules. If so, which referee is responsible for blowing the whistle in this situation? 7. Executed attack hit of a back-row player, the ball is with full circumference above the top of the net at the moment of contact, the back-row player touches the attacking line with the tip of his foot during the take-off.

answers.a: Fault; 2nd referee.
answers.b: Fault; 1st and 2nd referee.
answers.c: Fault; 1st referee.
answers.d: No fault.

question: Indicate whether the situations described below are against the rules. If so, which referee is responsible for blowing the whistle in this situation? 8. A player touches the antenna above the top of the net on the 2nd referee's side during an action of playing the ball.

answers.a: Fault; 1st and 2nd referee.
answers.b: Fault; 2nd referee.
answers.c: Fault; 1st referee.
answers.d: No fault.

question: The front-row players of the serving team stand together in a group directly in front of two receiving players. The captain of the opponent team asks the 1st referee to inform the group to stand so that his receiving players can see the serving player.

answers.a: The 1st referee does not have to respond to the captain's request, but he is allowed to point out the possible screen to team "A".
answers.b: The 1st referee is obliged to inform the group to line up individually.
answers.c: The 1st referee is not allowed to respond to the captain's request.

question: Player No. 8 is entered in the team list as the team captain. The registered coach (player No. 12) is substituted onto the court and, after a few rallies, requests a time-out (player No. 8 was also on the court at this moment).

answers.a: The referees reject the request. The first time without sanction, any further improper request will be sanction as delay.
answers.b: The referees reject the request and issue a sanction for delay.
answers.c: The referees accept the request and grant the time-out.

question: A blocker of the serving team stands close to the net during the serve and touches the opponent's court with part of his foot.

answers.a: The referee allows play to continue.
answers.b: The 2nd referee whistles a positional fault.
answers.c: The 1st referee whistles a positional fault.

question: Team "A" requests a substitution after the 1st referee's whistle for service. The buzzer has been pressed by the scorer. This happens the second time in the same match.

answers.a: The referees do not react to the coach's request because there is no delay.
answers.b: The 1st referee sanctions the team for delay after the rally.
answers.c: The referee stops the rally immediately and issues a delay sanction to the team.

question: The coach of team "A" makes a substitution and immediately afterwards takes a time-out. Before the game is continued, he requests another substitution. Can the referee authorise this ?

answers.a: No.
answers.b: Yes.

question: Continuation of the previous question: In the above case, may the coach take another time-out instead of the 2nd substitution without the game having continued after the 1st time-out?

answers.a: Yes.
answers.b: No.

question: During the match, the 2nd referee decides and whistles the following faults:

answers.a: Penetration into the opponent's court under the net.
answers.b: Four hits of a team.
answers.c: Block of a back-row player.
answers.d: Faults in playing the ball (catch, double touch).
answers.e: Net touch faults.
answers.f: Positional fault of the serving team.
answers.h: Illegal attack hit of a back-row player.
answers.i: Positional fault of the receiving team.

question: The starting line-up of the players at the beginning of the set is the following (starting from position 1): 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6. The last player on service was No. 1. The positions in the drawing are therefore:

answers.a: Correct regarding the front-row players, incorrect regarding the back-row players.
answers.b: Correct regarding the front-row and back-row players.
answers.c: Correct regarding the back-row players, incorrect regarding the front-row players.

question: The starting line-up of the players at the beginning of the set is the following (starting from position 1): 4, 9, 7, 2, 5 and 1. Player No. 5 is substituted by player No. 3. The libero replaces player No. 7. The last player to serve was player No. 2. The positions in the diagram are therefore:

answers.a: Correct.
answers.b: Wrong, between players 9 and 3.
answers.c: Wrong, between players 4 and 9.

question: The unsuccessful ball reception of team "B" flies outside the crossing space into the free zone of team "A". Libero "B" reaches the ball and plays it back outside the crossing space into his own court from the opponent's free zone, with overhand fingers. An attacker "B" plays the ball, which is completely higher than the top of the net, into the opponent's court. The referee whistles a fault for team "B" because the attack was completed after a pass from the libero with overhand fingers. Did he act correctly?

answers.a: Yes.
answers.b: No.

question: One player is ready for substitution in accordance with the rules, the other is busy looking for the substitution paddel by the players' bench. The referees allow one substitution, the second substitution is rejected because he was still searching after the completion of the first substitution. The referees allow play to continue without delay and without sanction. Is this correct?

answers.a: Yes.
answers.b: No.

question: During a poor reception, the setter penetrating from the second line (back-row player) of team "A" participates in the two-man block. The block was executed by the blocker of team "A" (front-row player). The referees allowed team "A" three more hits. Did they act correctly?

answers.a: Yes.
answers.b: No.

question: On the third touch of the ball by Team "A" over the top of the net, the ball is touched simultaneously by the attacker "A" and the blocking player "B" (fully in the playing area of Team "A"). Subsequently, the ball touches the antenna. The 1st referee whistles a fault of the block player. Is this correct?

answers.a: No.
answers.b: Yes.

question: The 1st referee decides that team A wins the rally. Team A immediately executes a substitution. The 1st referee then corrects his decision and gives the win of the rally to team B. Which statements apply to the situation that has arisen?

answers.a: Team A may 'cancel' the substitution (i.e. they may reverse it without consequences and take their previous line-up).
answers.b: Team A must play the next rally with the substituted player.
answers.c: Team A may not 'cancel' the substitution, but may re-substitute the player immediately (if this is 'legally' possible).

question: A back-row player of team A attacks and hits the ball past a block of three players of team B into the court of B. The ball is completely higher than the top of the net at the moment of the attack hit, the block of B does not touch the ball after the attack hit. The back-row player of A touches the attack line during the take-off; the libero participates in the block of B. Which action of the referees is correct?

answers.a: The referees decide on point for B due to the fault of the back-row player.
answers.b: The referees decide on point for A because the libero is involved in a block attempt.
answers.c: The referees decide on double fault.

question: Team A has the libero in position 5 and must receive. A wins the rally and rotates by one position. The libero leaves the court and the replaced middle blocker returns to the court. The 1st referee then penalises the new service player from A in position 1 with a red card. As a consequence, the right to serve changes to team B and B receives a point. A now wants to replace the player in position 1 (the other middle blocker) with the libero as usual. How do the referees decide?

answers.a: The referees allow the replacement. A therefore plays the next rally with the libero in reception on position 1.
answers.b: The referees do not allow the replacement. A therefore plays the next rally with the middle blocker in reception on position 1.
answers.c: The referees authorise the replacement. This replacement is entered in the 'Remarks' section of the score sheet as an exceptional replacement. A therefore plays the next rally with the libero in reception on position 1.

question: The team captain is substituted and another player becomes game captain. Which statements are correct?

answers.a: As game captain, this player may submit a request from a team-mate to the 1st referee.
answers.b: If this player is back in the starting line-up at the beginning of the next set, but the team captain is not, this player is then again 'automatically' game captain.
answers.c: As game captain, this player may request the 1st referee to check the height of the net.

question: Team A wins the rally. Immediately afterwards, there is simultaneous rude conduct by one player from each team. In what order are the penalties (red cards) given to these players?

answers.a: First to the player of team A.
answers.b: First to the player of team B.
answers.c: The order is at the discretion of the 1st referee.

question: After the whistle for the serve, the 1st referee notices that one team has seven players on the court; a libero has come onto the court and the respective replacement player has not seen this. The 1st referee stops the rally. How should he then proceed?

answers.a: He sanctions this team for delaying the game.
answers.b: He decides on a positional fault of this team.
answers.c: He allows the replacement player to leave the court and restarts the rally again, blowing his whistle, without any consequences.

question: Team A serves. Team B wins the rally. The next service player of this team according to the rotational order goes to serve. Before the 1st referee blows the whistle, the 2nd referee notices that this player was standing in the wrong position during A's previous service. Which statements are true?

answers.a: Team B is sanctioned for a rotational fault.
answers.b: Team B is not sanctioned, the match continues without intervention of the referees.
answers.c: Team B is sanctioned for a positional fault.

question: In the NLB, a substitute player enters the substitution zone. After the 2nd referee has blown the whistle, the coach gives the substitute player a different numbered paddle because the player has accidentally entered the substitution zone with the wrong paddle. What is the procedure?

answers.a: The 2nd referee rejects the request for substitution, the 1st referee imposes a sanction for delay.
answers.b: The substitution is carried out without consequences according to the original numbered paddle.
answers.c: The 1st referee rejects the request for substitution and imposes a sanction for delay.
answers.d: The substitution is carried out without consequences accordint to the new numbered paddle.

question: A substitute player enters the substitution zone at the moment of the 1st referee's whistle for service. Which statements are correct?

answers.a: The action of the substitute player is - in any case - to be sanctioned as a delay.
answers.b: The action of the substitute player constitutes an improper request.
answers.c: The 2nd referee should stop the rally and the request for substitution should be accepted.

question: The libero of team A enters the court. The 1st referee blows the whistle to start the next rally and then blows the whistle again shortly after the serve because a second ball rolls into the court. Which statements apply to the resulting situation?

answers.a: Team A may replace the libero in any case.
answers.b: Team B may request a time-out (1st or 2nd time-out in this set).
answers.c: Team A may replace the libero if he has been injured in the meantime.

question: A team plays with 2 liberos (L1 and L2). Libero L1 is disqualified. Which statements apply to the rest of the game?

answers.a: If libero L2 is also disqualified later, the team can re-designate a new libero.
answers.b: If libero L2 is later expelled, the team can re-designate a new libero.
answers.c: If libero L2 is injured later, the team can re-designate a new libero.

question: A front-row player makes a 'one-man-block', touching the opponent's attack ball. In which cases may the team then hit the ball three more times?

answers.a: The blocker reaches over the top of the net with one hand, the attack ball only touches his head below the top of the net.
answers.b: The blocker does not reach with any part of his body over the top of the net, the attack ball touches one of his hands.
answers.c: The blocker reaches over the top of the net with one hand, the attack ball touches this hand and immediately afterwards his head.

question: At the moment of the serve, a back-row player of the receiving team is at the same height as his front-row player. Which statements are correct?

answers.a: No fault if the back foot of the back-row player is behind the front foot of the front-row player.
answers.b: Is counted as a fault even if the server commits a fault at the moment of hitting the ball.
answers.c: This is not a fault, because the back-row player is not completely in front of the front-row player.
answers.d: Is counted as a fault if the serve becomes faulty after the ball has been hit.

question: The coach requests a time-out from the 2nd referee for the first time, but the 1st referee has already whistled for the serve. The 2nd referee interrupts the rally. What should be done if the team has not yet been sanctioned for delay?

answers.a: The team is granted the requested time-out.
answers.b: The 1st referee ignores the whistle of the 2nd referee and allows play to continue on the condition that the players have not interrupted the rally.
answers.c: The request is rejected without sanction and the 1st re-starts the rally again, blowing the whistle.
answers.d: The time-out is not granted and the team is sanctioned for delay. The right to serve is granted again.

question: Which requests for regular game interruptions are improper?

answers.a: The request is made at the same time as the 1st referee whistles for the execution of the serve.
answers.b: The coach requests a time-out after a substitution without the game having been resumed in the meantime.
answers.c: The coach requests a time-out and then, without the game having been resumed, a substitution of two players by sending the substitutes to the substitution zone at the same time.
answers.d: The game captain requests a time-out because the coach is in the warm-up area.

question: What are the correct characteristics of a volleyball?

answers.a: Circumference 66 – 68 cm; weight 260 – 280 g; inside pressure 0.275 – 0.325 kg/cm2
answers.b: Circumference 65 – 67 cm; weight 260 – 280 g; inside pressure 0.300 – 0.325 kg/cm2
answers.c: Circumference 66 – 68 cm; weight 250 – 270 g; inside pressure 0.300 – 0.325 kg/cm2
answers.d: Circumference 65 – 67 cm; weight 260 – 280 g; inside pressure 0.275 – 0.325 kg/cm2

question: What are the correct dimensions of the extensions of the attack lines?

answers.a: Per extension 5 line pieces of 15 cm each with 25 cm spacing.
answers.b: Per extension 5 line pieces of 20 cm each with 15 cm spacing.
answers.c: Per extension 5 line pieces of 15 cm each with 20 cm spacing.
answers.d: Per extension 5 line pieces of 20 cm each with 25 cm spacing.
answers.e: Per extension 5 line pieces of 20 cm each with 20 cm spacing.

question: At official competitions of Swiss Volley in the category National (NLA, NLB and 1L) the jerseys of the players must be numbered from 1 to ...

answers.a: 18 (NLA, NLB and 1L)
answers.b: 20 (NLA)
answers.d: 24 (NLA, NLB and 1L)
answers.e: 99 (NLA)

question: [htmlThe coach hands the line-up sheet to the 2nd referee shortly before the start of the set. After entering the starting line-up on the score sheet (e-scoresheet), the 2nd referee checks the line-up on the court. He realises that player #5 is on the court instead of the player #8, who is listed on the line-up sheet. How do the referees decide correctly?

answers.a: At the coach's request, the scorer changes #8 to #5 on the score sheet and the team is sanctioned for delay.
answers.b: Player #8 must enter the court according to the line-up sheet. The coach can then request a legal substitution against player #5. The team is sanctioned for delay.
answers.c: At the coach's request, the scorer changes #8 to #5 in the score sheet, without any sanction.
answers.d: According to the line-up sheet, player #8 should be on the court. However, the coach can request a legal substitution against player #5. There is no sanction.

question: Which of the listed situations is seen as a completed rally?

answers.a: During a rally, a ball rolls from the ball retriever into the court and game is stopped by the referee.
answers.b: The ball lands outside the opponent's court after the service.
answers.c: When the ball is touched above the net at the same time ("holding ball"/ball stops), the ball touches the antenna.
answers.d: When serving, at the moment of the service hit, both the positional fault of the serving team and the receiving team are whistled at the same time.
answers.e: The player is expelled (yellow and red card jointly).
answers.f: After a simultaneous contact of the ball over the net where the ball is "held", the ball goes "out".
answers.g: When serving, at the moment of the service hit, a positional fault of the receiving team and a rotation fault of the serving team are whistled simultaneously.
answers.h: A team receives a delay penalty (red card).

question: Which team compositions are permitted in the NLA and NLB?

answers.a: 9 "normal" players plus 2 liberos.
answers.b: 12 players, no libero.
answers.c: 14 players, no libero.
answers.d: 12 "normal" players plus 1 libero.
answers.e: 11 "normal" players plus 2 liberos.

question: The coach is disqualified. Which statements are correct?

answers.a: The assistant coach can not take over the duties of the coach, as the coach leaves the team due to a sanction.
answers.b: The assistant coach may take over the duties of the coach. If this happens, the game captain is subsequently not allowed to request time-outs.
answers.c: The game captain may subsequently request time-outs in any case.
answers.d: The assistant coach may take over the duties of the coach. If this is not the case, the game captain may subsequently request time-outs.

question: A team requests a time-out after the 1st referee has blown his whistle for service. This is the first unauthorised request by the team. Which statements are correct?

answers.a: If there is no delay, the 2nd referee rejects the request. At the end of the rally, the improper request is entered in the appropriate section of the sanction part. No sanction is imposed.
answers.b: The rally is stopped immediately and the improper request is entered in the new section of the sanction part. No sanction is imposed.
answers.c: The rally is stopped immediately and the team is sanctioned for delay. The improper request is entered in the new section in the sanction part.

question: In which cases of incorrect conduct should a disqualification be issued?

answers.a: In the event of a physical attack.
answers.b: In the event of aggressive behaviour.
answers.c: In the event of a defamatory gesture.

question: The coach, facing the 2nd referee, executes the hand signal "substitution". A substitute player comes running from behind the players' bench, looks for the correct substitution paddle and then appears in the substitution zone. Which statements are correct?

answers.a: The 2nd referee confirms the substitution request at the moment when the substitute player enters the substitution zone (provided that the 1st referee has not yet blown the whistle for service).
answers.b: The 2nd referee blows the whistle when he sees the coach's hand signal, the substitution is subsequently rejected, the team receives a delay sanction.
answers.c: The 2nd referee does not respond to the coach's hand signal, the substitution is rejected, and the team receives a delay sanction.
answers.d: If the substitution request is confirmed but the substitute player is not ready to play when entering the substitution zone, the substitution will not be allowed and the team will receive a delay sanction.

question: During a rally, a player from team A is injured. The 1st referee stops the rally immediately and decides to "replay". Which statements apply in this situation?

answers.a: Neither team can request a time-out.
answers.b: If the player is unable to continue playing, he must be substituted.
answers.c: If team B has not made a substitution before the start of the interrupted rally, it may now request a substitution.

question: Between the 1st referee's whistle for service and the execution of the serve, the opposing team carries out a libero replacement. The rally is stopped immediately and the team concerned is sanctioned with a delay. Is this in accordance with the rules?

answers.a: Yes, in any case.
answers.b: Only if this late replacement action occurs repeatedly with the same team.
answers.c: Whenever this team had already received a delay sanction.

question: After receiving the ball, it flies below the net towards the opponent's court. Before the ball has completely crossed the vertical plane of the net, the setter plays it back to his own court. Is this allowed?

answers.a: Only if the ball is touched in the player's own side of the court.
answers.b: No, if the ball has already partially crossed the vertical plane of the net.
answers.c: Yes, the ball may also be touched in the opponent's side of the court.

question: A player touches the opponent's court. Which statements are true?

answers.a: The touch is not a fault if it happens with the foot and part of the foot is on the center line.
answers.b: The touch is not a fault if it happens with the knee and it does not affect the opponent's play.
answers.c: The touch is not a fault if it happens with the complete hand and it does not affect the opponent's play.

question: A back-row player takes part in a "block of three". In which cases does this lead to a fault?

answers.a: He is not the libero, and the opponent's attack ball does not touch any of the blocking players.
answers.b: He is the libero, and the opponent's attack ball does not touch any of the blocking players.
answers.c: The opponent's attack ball touches another player of the block, but not him.

question: A player touches the net. In which cases is this a fault?

answers.a: The player touches the white band at the top of the net outside the antenna.
answers.b: The setter clearly touches the net below the white band at the top of the net while passing the ball.
answers.c: An attacker grazes the top band of the net with his hitting hand after his attack hit.

question: At the moment of service hit, a player touches the center line with one foot without crossing it. Is this a fault?

answers.a: Only, if the foot crosses the axis of the center line.
answers.b: No.
answers.c: Yes, in any case.

question: After a bad reception, the ball flies outside the antenna into the opponent's free zone. A player plays the ball back into his own court. Which statements are correct?

answers.a: The player returning the ball is never allowed to touch the opponent's court.
answers.b: The player returning the ball is not allowed to touch the opponent's court, unless his foot is on or above the centre line.
answers.c: The playing back is only legal if the ball crosses the plane of the net totally through the external space on the same side of the net.
answers.d: The player returning the ball may jump onto the opposing team's bench.

question: The ball received by team A flies higher than the top of the net in the direction of the opponent's court. A front-row player from team B jumps to block. Team A's setter reaches the ball just before it crosses the plane of the net and, as he passes the ball to one of his attackers, the blocker touches his hand. Is this a mistake by the blocker?

answers.a: Only if the touch of the hand "spoils" the pass.
answers.b: Yes, in any case.
answers.c: No, because he did not touch the ball.

question: A team plays with 2 liberos. In the 2nd set, the coach wants to replace the acting libero with the second libero. Which statements are correct?

answers.a: This replacement is possible without further justification.
answers.b: This replacement is possible, but can only be reversed in the next set if the new libero is injured.
answers.c: The acting libero can not be on the court when this replacement is done.

question: A team is playing with one libero. During the match, the coach wants to re-designate another player (who is not on the court) as the new libero. Which statements are true?

answers.a: The re-designation is not permitted under any circumstances, as the team does not have a second libero.
answers.b: If the libero is injured, the re-desigation is allowed directly on the court.
answers.c: The re-designation is permitted directly on the court in any case.

question: The libero enters the court for player No. 5. At the next "swap back", player No. 7 enters the court for the libero. What should be done when this is noticed?

answers.a: The incorrect line-up is not corrected, a substitution (7 for 5) is noted and the team receives a delay sanction.
answers.b: If this is noticed before the start of the next rally, the incorrect line-up must be corrected and the team receives a delay sanction.
answers.c: If this is only noticed after the serve has been executed, the incorrect line-up must be corrected, all points scored by the team since the incorrect replacement are cancelled and the opponent is awarded a point and the serve.

question: The server executes the service outside the service zone. At the same time, the 2nd referee calls for a positional fault by the receiving team. The 1st referee decides ...

answers.a: ... to replay.
answers.b: ... a fault of the serving team.

question: During the interval between two sets, the players of team A want to warm up with the ball on the court. The referee has forbidden this. Did he act correctly?

answers.a: No.
answers.b: Yes.

question: The 2nd referee does not turn up for a championship match. No referee is present among the spectators. The 1st referee ...

answers.a: ... directs the match with the agreement of both teams together with a coach of the teams involved.
answers.b: ... is not allowed to direct the match alone and goes home again.
answers.c: ... directs the match alone.

question: The home team causes the match to start late (with verifiable and sufficient justification). The postponement of the new start of the match in the national leagues shall not exceed ...

answers.a: ... 15 minutes if the match in question is followed by a second match in the same sports hall and if the duration of use of the hall is limited.
answers.b: ... 60 minutes.
answers.c: ... 30 minutes.

question: The two team captains cannot agree on a joint warm-up session at the net. The separate official warm-up (5 minutes each) starts with ...

answers.a: ... the home team.
answers.b: ... the visiting team.
answers.c: ... the team that has the first reception.
answers.d: ... the team that has the first service.

question: For the judgement of an attack hit by a back-row player in the front zone, the following is decisive:

answers.a: The height of contact with the ball in relation to the top of the net.
answers.b: The position of the ball in relation to the top of the net.

question: The block is executed, ...

answers.a: ... if the ball has fully entered the opponent's court after being blocked.
answers.b: ... whenever the ball is touched by a blocking player.

question: The attack hit is considered executed ...

answers.a: ... when the ball has completely crossed the vertical plane of the net.
answers.b: ... when the ball is touched by an opponent.
answers.c: ... at the moment the attacking player touches the ball.

question: An attack hit is understood to be ...

answers.a: ... all actions in which the ball is played above the top of the net in the direction of the opponent, except service and block.
answers.b: ... all actions in which the ball is played in the direction of the opponent, except service and block.

question: An injured player cannot be substituted either legally or exceptionally. The referee ...

answers.a: ... grants the player three minutes of recovery time, but not more than once for the same player in the match.
answers.b: ... declares the team incomplete and ends the match.

question: A team refuses to play at the start of the third set. The 1st referee declares the team in default. What should be done next?

answers.a: The opposing team will be given the sets needed to win the match. The team in default keeps its points and sets.
answers.b: The final score is 0:3 for the match and 0:25 for each set.

question: The scorer does not receive the team line-up for the next set in time. What should be done next?

answers.a: For this reason, he automatically uses the team line-up from the previous set.
answers.b: He reports this to the 2nd referee without delay.

question: The coach requests a 7th substitution for the second time. The referee judges this as ...

answers.a: ... a repeated improper request and imposes a sanction for delay.
answers.b: ... the first improper request and does not impose any further sanctions.

question: The ball flies towards the playing surface. A player plays the ball in such a way that the ball touches both his fingers and the playing surface. The 1st referee does not see this, but the 2nd referee can recognise the situation. What is the correct procedure?

answers.a: The 2nd referee whistles the contact of the ball with the playing surface.
answers.b: The referees allow play to continue. After the 2nd referee has informed the 1st referee about the situation at the end of the rally, the 1st referee decides to replay the rally.

question: A back-row player "A" takes off behind the attack line. The ball is above the front zone "A" after the opponent's service and is completely higher than the top of the net. The back-row player "A" plays the ball into the court of "B". How to decide?

answers.a: The 1st referee blows the whistle, team "B" receives the ball.
answers.b: The 1st referee blows the whistle, team "A" receives the ball.
answers.c: The 1st referee does not blow the whistle, the action is correct.

question: After the end of a rally, player "A" asks the referee for permission to tie his footwear. This does not result in an effective delay. What is the correct procedure?

answers.a: The referee decides for a delay of team "A".
answers.b: The referee has the player tie his footwear.

question: Team A is serving and commits a rotational fault, e.g. the wrong player serves. At the same time, the receiving team B commits a positional fault. How is the decision to be made?

answers.a: Point for team A.
answers.b: Double fault, replay of the rally.
answers.c: Point for team B.

question: After a time-out, the wrong player prepares to serve. The referee authorizes the service. Before the service is executed, the team realises the mistake and the correct player goes to serve. The 1st referee whistles again to authorize the service. Has the referee acted correctly?

answers.a: No.
answers.b: Yes.

question: After the 1st referee has already blown his whistle to authorize the service, the 2nd referee reacts late to a request for a time-out and also blows his whistle. How must the decision be made?

answers.a: The 1st referee must decide on a double fault and then restart the rally. The time-out is not granted and a sanction for delay is imposed.
answers.b: The 1st referee must decide on a double fault. If the request for a time-out is renewed, the time-out is granted.
answers.c: As the rally has has already been authorized and started, the service must be executed within 8 seconds. The time-out is not granted.

question: After team A has scored several points on its own serve, a rotational fault is detected for this team. What should be done?

answers.a: Only the last point scored by team A is cancelled and credited to the opponent, the other points are regarded as "played" and are retained.
answers.b: If the moment of the fault can be determined, team A is deprived of all points since the moment the fault occurred. The opponent's points remain valid and the last rally is awarded to the opponent.

question: Team A has 7 players available, including one libero. In the 2nd set, one player (not the libero) is injured and cannot continue playing. How does the match continue?

answers.a: The libero can be substituted immediately for the injured player.
answers.b: Team A is declared incomplete for the 2nd set and loses it. As no other player is available, the libero can take part in the match as a "normal" player in the 3rd set after an exceptional substitution.
answers.c: Team A is declared incomplete and loses the entire game.

question: Which penalties also result in the opponent gaining a point?

answers.a: Yellow card.
answers.b: Red card.
answers.c: Yellow and red cards jointly.

question: In the 1st set, team A has received a delay warning. In the following set, another player of this team commits a similar offence. How must the referee decide?

answers.a: As both delays were committed in two different sets, a further delay warning will be issued.
answers.b: Sanctions for delay are personal sanctions, therefore a further delay warning will be issued.
answers.c: Sanctions for delay are team sanctions and are effective for the entire match, therefore a "delay penalty" will be issued, which results in a point and service to the opponent.

question: Due to the unfavorable architecture of the sports hall, there is an area in the middle of the hall that cannot be seen by the referees at full height. A high ball is played, which briefly leaves the referees' field of vision. In the judgement of the first referee, the ball does not necessarily touch the ceiling, but it cannot be ruled out. How should he decide?

answers.a: Since a fault cannot be ruled out according to the referee's assessment, he must decide on double fault and replay of the rally in any case.
answers.b: Play continues because none of the referees noticed an obvious fault.
answers.c: Only if one of the teams complains about a fault, the rally must be stopped. The referee decides to award a point (and service) to the team that did not cause the fault.
answers.d: Only if one of the teams complains about a fault, the rally must be stopped. The referee decides on double fault and replay of the rally.

question: A front-row player executes a block. In doing so, he touches the ball in the opponent's playing space at the same time as the opponent's attack hit. The attack is executed by the opposing team by a back-row player within the front zone completely above the top of the net. How is the decision to be made?

answers.a: Double fault, replay of the rally.
answers.b: Fault of the attacker.
answers.c: Fault of the block player.

question: Continuation of the previous question: What is the correct decision if the block player is a back-row player?

answers.a: Fault of the attacker.
answers.b: Double fault, replay of the rally.
answers.c: Fault of the block player.

question: The ball is passed by the libero with overhand fingers, the attacker hits the ball above net height. This is permitted if the libero, while setting the ball, is located in the ...

answers.a: back zone and the attack is executed within the front zone by a front-row player.
answers.b: back zone and the attacker jumps off at least behind the attack line.
answers.c: front zone and the attacker jumps off at least behind the attack line.
answers.d: front zone and the attack is executed within the front zone.

question: At the score of 24:20 for Team A, the opposing team hits an attack ball out. The referees fail to recognise that a blocker from Team A has touched the ball. The 1st referee decides on a point for Team A and ends the set. Only now does the blocker admit to touching the ball. However, the 1st referee sticks to his decision, stating that the set has already ended and that the decision can therefore no longer be changed. Is he acting correctly?

answers.a: No, the rally should be awarded to Team B.
answers.b: No, the rally should be replayed.
answers.c: Yes.

question: What does this hand signal from the referee mean?

answers.a: Attack hit fault by a libero.
answers.b: Blocking fault by the Libero.
answers.c: Attack hit fault on an overhand finger pass by the libero in his/her front zone.
answers.d: Illegal direct return of the opponent's serve.
answers.e: Illegally reaching beyond the net.
answers.f: Attack hit fault by a back-row player.
answers.g: Blocking fault by a back-row player.

question: If there is a discrepancy between the line-up sheet and the current line-up of players (including libero) before the first service in the first set, the following applies:

answers.a: The scorer must correct the line-up sheet and the score sheet.
answers.b: The players must line up according to the line-up sheet, without further sanctions.
answers.c: The players must line up according to the line-up sheet and the team is sanctioned with a delay warning.

question: Concerning the first hit of a team, it is a fault if ...

answers.a: ... the ball bounced off one's own block and is deliberately played with the foot of one of the block players.
answers.b: ... the ball bounces back into the net from the attacker's head (attacker completely below the top of the net at the moment of contact) after an opponent's block and the same player continues to play the ball.
answers.c: ... the ball is played with an overhand finger pass while receiving the opponent’s attack hit and thus touches both hands in succession, during one action.

question: After the server has tossed the ball up, the ball touches both the server's foot and the floor. How does the 1st referee decide?

answers.a: The serving team receives a delay sanction and the 1st referee allows the same player to serve again.
answers.b: This action was a service fault.
answers.c: A second service attempt must be executed without delay within 8 seconds after the 1st referee's whistle.

question: The server inadvertently bounces the ball on his foot after the 1st referee has authorised the service, after which the ball rolls into the court:

answers.a: The action is not a service fault. The player has the right to execute the service within 8 seconds.
answers.b: The 1st referee authorises another service attempt.
answers.c: This is a service fault.

question: During the 1st set, the 2nd referee notices that the coach is sitting at the end of the players' bench furthest away from the scorer's table. The 2nd referee demands that the coach takes up his correct position on the bench. The coach refuses to do so.

answers.a: The 1st referee insists that the coach takes up the correct position on the bench or stands up and moves in the free zone in front of his team bench.
answers.b: The 1st referee gives the coach a yellow card and insists that the coach takes up the correct position on the bench or stands up and moves in the free zone in front of his team bench.
answers.c: The 1st referee ignores the coach. It is the 2nd referee's duty to control the team bench.

question: Is it a fault if a player reaches over the net with his hands and hits the ball instead of blocking it?

answers.a: No, if the initial contact took place in the opponent’s playing area.
answers.b: No, if the initial contact took place in the own playing area.
answers.c: Yes, if the initial contact took place in the opponent’s playing area.
answers.d: Yes, if the initial contact took place in the own playing area.

question: Team "A" wins the rally and prepares to serve. After the whistle, the server throws the ball in the air so that it touches the ceiling. Without dropping the ball, the server hits the ball and serves. The 1st referee:

answers.a: Allows the execution of the serve.
answers.b: Must whistle and the serve is repeated.
answers.c: Team "B" wins the rally – illegal execution of the serve.

question: After the first hit, the receiving team "A" plays the ball into the free zone of the opponent "B", whereby the ball has completely crossed the vertical plane of the net in the external space. One of the players of team "A" wants to reach this ball, using the net post to quickly change direction. He then plays the ball back into his own playing space and the ball flies completely through the external space on the same side of the court. The referee:

answers.a: Whistles a fault by the receiving team "A".
answers.b: Allows the rally to continue.
answers.c: Replays the rally.

question: A team is playing without coach or assistant coach. The team captain is player No. 1. In the 2nd set, player No. 1 sits on the team bench and requests a time-out from the 2nd referee. The 2nd referee:

answers.a: Authorises the time-out.
answers.b: Will authorise the time-out and warn the team captain, as only the game captain has the right to request regular game interruptions.
answers.c: Does not authorise the time-out – only the game captain has the right to request time-outs and substitutions.

question: A player of team "A" goes to serve. The service player executes the service within the prescribed time, but the ball flies into the net, close below the top of the net. The net meshes tear and the ball flies through the net into the playing court of team "B".

answers.a: The referee decides to replay the rally.
answers.b: Team "B" wins the rally.
answers.c: The referee allows play to continue.

question: The position of the players is determined and controlled depending on ...

answers.a: ... the position of their bodies in relation to the plane of the net.
answers.b: ... the position of their feet touching the ground.
answers.c: ... the position of a part of their body that touches the floor (hands, feet, knees, etc.).

question: A player from team "A" executes an attack hit. The ball flies near the antenna in the direction of the external space. A player from team "B" blocks this attack in the opponent's space outside the antenna. After the block, the ball touches the floor of the playing area of team "A".

answers.a: The rally must be replayed.
answers.b: Fault by the blocking team "B".
answers.c: Fault by the attacking team "A".

question: Before the start of the match, the libero registered in the e-scoresheet is injured. The coach asks for the libero to be re-designated. The new libero should be the player No. 1, who is already entered in the line-up in the e-scoresheet for the first set. How to proceed?

answers.a: The referee allows the re-designation before the start of the match. The player No. 1 is substituted legally (score 0:0). The number of the new libero must be entered in the e-scoresheet under remarks.
answers.b: The referee rejects the request and the re-designation can take place after the first rally of the first set. All circumstances must be entered in the e scoresheet under remarks.
answers.c: The referee allows the re-designation, the line-up in the e-scoresheet for the first set is changed, and the player No. 1 can play as the new libero in the first set. All circumstances must be entered in the e-scoresheet under remarks.

question: In the 2nd set, player No. 10 substitutes player No. 2 of team "A". In the same set, player no. 2 is substituted back for player No. 10. Later (in the same set), player No. 2 is disqualified. The referee:

answers.a: Refuses an "exceptional substitution" and declares the team incomplete for this set. The match continues with the 3rd set. Player No. 2 cannot continue to play in this match.
answers.b: Refuses an "exceptional substitution", declares the team incomplete for the match and ends the match. The incomplete team keeps its points and sets.
answers.c: Authorises an "exceptional substitution" of player No. 2 by any player sitting on the bench. Player No. 2 must go to the team dressing room for the remainder of the match.

question: After hitting the ball, the player touches the ropes that attach the net to the post.

answers.a: No fault is committed, provided that this contact does not interfere with play.
answers.b: The referee may not interrupt the rally under any circumstances.
answers.c: The referee whistles the fault "contact of the player with the net".

question: After the blocking players of team "A" land, one of them remains on the ground with a twisted ankle. The players of the opposing team are successful in playing the ball and prepare the next attack. The referee:

answers.a: Must stop play immediately and allow medical assistance to enter the court. The rally is replayed.
answers.b: Does not stop the rally.
answers.c: Stops the rally and issues a time-out for team "A".

question: Player No. 3 of team "A" attacks, the ball is in the front zone and is completely higher than the top of the net. The attack ball lands on the opponent's court. The referee decides in favour of team "A". After the players have lined up correctly again, the referee realises that player No. 3 is a back-row player. The referee:

answers.a: Does not change his decision.
answers.b: Changes his decision and team "B" wins the previous rally.
answers.c: Decides to replay the rally.

question: The front-row setter of team "A" tries to play a ball that comes from a team-mate. He jumps up and hits the ball over the net with one hand. At the moment he touches the ball, his hand is in the opponent's space. The ball played in this way flies on the setter’s side, parallel to the net, in the direction of an attacker from team "A". The block of team "B" touches the ball before the attacker of team "A", so that the attacker "A" cannot execute the attack. The 1st referee:

answers.a: Whistles the fault from block "B".
answers.b: Whistles the fault from setter "A".
answers.c: Doesn't whistle, no fault has been made.

question: The receiving team is positioned incorrectly at the moment of the service. The server touches the end line when jumping to serve. The referee:

answers.a: Decides the rally in favour of the serving team – positional fault by the receiving team.
answers.b: Decides on "double fault" - the rally is replayed.
answers.c: Decides the rally in favour of the receiving team – fault by the server.

question: A player touches the playing surface of the opponent with his fingers under the net with his palm above the centre line. The referee:

answers.a: Does not whistle because this penetration does not interfere with the opponent.
answers.b: Whistles only in the event that the palm of the hand is not in contact with the centre line.
answers.c: Whistles an unauthorised penetration under the net into the opponent's court.

question: The libero of team "A" makes an overhand finger pass in the front zone. An attacking player plays the ball, which is completely higher than the top of the net, towards the opponent. The attacking ball touches the opponent's block, in which a back-row player also participates. After the block touch, the ball still remains in play. The referee blows the whistle:

answers.a: The team "B" (block) wins the rally.
answers.b: The team "A" (attack) wins the rally.
answers.c: Rally is replayed, double fault.

question: After two opponents touch the ball over the net at the same time (both players try to hit the ball), the ball lands on the floor completely outside the side line of team "A". The rally:

answers.a: Must be replayed.
answers.b: Is won by team "A".
answers.c: Is won by team "B".
answers.d: Must be whistled in any case, if the contact was completed above the opponent court.

question: Before serving, the game captain asks the referee to check the opponent's line-up. The referee:

answers.a: Rejects the request without any other consequences.
answers.b: Sanctions the team for delay.
answers.c: Informs the captain whether the opponent is correctly lined-up or not. The referee is not permitted to show the opponent's line-up sheet or to say which player is a front-row or back-row player.

question: A player of team "A" blocks the attack of team "B", so that the ball flies through the space of team "B" and then under the net into the space of the blocking team "A". The referee:

answers.a: Whistles a fault of team "A" (block).
answers.b: Replays the rally.
answers.c: Whistles a fault of team "B" (attack).

question: If a player is injured during the match, procedures are as follows:

answers.a: The player must be granted a recovery time of 3 minutes immediately and in any case, but only once per player in a match.
answers.b: The team must first use its time-outs, only then will it be granted a recovery time of 3 minutes, but only once per player in a match.
answers.c: The player must be granted a recovery time of 3 minutes if he cannot be substituted either legally or exceptionally, but only once per player in a match.

question: After a bad reception by team "A", the ball flies directly towards the net. A player from team "B" clearly moves his arms with the intention of hitting the ball through the net into the opponent's court, so that the receiving team has no chance of playing the ball. The referee decides:

answers.a: Rally is replayed – "double fault".
answers.b: Team "A" wins the rally – net fault of team "B".
answers.c: Team "B" wins the rally – ball "in".

question: After a coach has been disqualified, the following must be observed:

answers.a: Only the team captain can assume the coach’s functions.
answers.b: The assistant coach can assume the coach's functions as soon as the game captain has informed the referee.
answers.c: The assistant coach automatically assumes the coach’s functions.

question: Coach "A" requests a substitution in accordance with the rules. When the player is already in the substitution zone, he cancels his request. The referees accept this and penalise team "A" for unsportsmanlike conduct. Is this correct?

answers.a: Yes.
answers.b: No.

question: In which case may a player block the service?

answers.a: Only if the ball remains in the blocker's half of the court after touching the block.
answers.b: The opponent's serve must not be blocked.

question: Two faults were committed by opponents at the same time, the 1st referee decides on DOUBLE FAULT. Coach "A" requests a substitution.

answers.a: The substitution will be granted.
answers.b: The substitution will be rejected. It will be considered as improper request.
answers.c: The substitution will be rejected. The team will not receive a sanction.

question: Team "A" plays the ball in a way that it touches the 2nd referee. The 1st referee decides:

answers.a: Rally will be replayed.
answers.b: Fault of team "A".

question: After landing of the block players of Team "A", one of the block players remains severely injured on the floor. Immediately after the injury, the block rebound touches the court. What is the procedure?

answers.a: The 1st referee stops the rally immediately and allows medical assistance to enter the court. The rally is replayed.
answers.b: At the end of the rally (ball touches the floor), the 1st referee allows medical assistance to enter the court. Team "B" is awarded the point and the right to serve.

question: During a time-out, the substitute players use a volleyball in the warm-up area. The referees:

answers.a: May allow the use of a heavy medicine ball or rubber ropes.
answers.b: Allow players to warm up with the ball.
answers.c: Prohibit the use of a volleyball.

question: During a playing action in the free zone in front of the team bench "A", a player "A" collides with his own coach standing in the authorised area in such a way that he can no longer reach the ball. What is the procedure?

answers.a: Fault by team "A".
answers.b: The rally is replayed.

question: Team discipline during the match is within the responsibility of:

answers.a: The coach only.
answers.b: The team captain and the coach in equal shares.
answers.c: The team captain only.

question: In which areas is the coach of a team allowed to move freely during the match?

answers.a: He may stand in the service zone.
answers.b: He may in any case move in the free zone from the extension of the attack line up to the end of the free zone.
answers.c: He may move in the free zone from the extension of the attack line up to the warm-up area, provided that the warm-up area is situated in the corner of the Competition Control Area.
answers.d: He may stand or walk within the free zone in the area in front of his team bench.
answers.e: He may in any case move in the free zone from the extension of the attack line up to the end of his/her team’s court.

question: After a long and intense rally, a player waves to a quickmopper to come onto the court and wipe away a wet spot on the floor. How do the referees react?

answers.a: If, in the opinion of the 1st referee, there is a need to wipe up a wet spot, the 1st referee will allow the quickmoppers to come onto the field and will authorise wiping.
answers.b: If the 1st referee is of the opinion that there is no need to bring the quickmoppers onto the court and that this is only intended to delay the game, he will impose a delay sanction.
answers.c: The 2nd referee has to control the work of the quickmoppers and only he is allowed to send them onto the court.
answers.d: If the 1st referee issues a delay sanction, he calls the captain to him and informs him by showing the corresponding card and hand signal.

question: After the second touch of the ball by team A, the ball crosses the plane of the net outside the antenna into the opponent's free zone. Player #5 of team A runs after the ball to return it to his own half of the court. At this moment, the 2nd referee whistles for a net fault by team B and team A wins the rally. Are the referees acting correctly?

answers.a: Yes, as long as the opposing player caused the net contact during an action of playing the ball, this decision is correct.
answers.b: Yes, in any case.
answers.c: No.

question: At the moment when the correct service player tosses and hits the ball, both teams stand within their court, but not according to the rotational order. How should the referees decide?

answers.a: Fault of the receiving team.
answers.b: Fault of the serving team.
answers.c: Replay of the rally.

question: What options has the 1st referee if he is of the opinion that some players of the serving team are holding their hands above the head during service?

answers.a: He can penalise this as a mistake.
answers.b: He can sanction this as a delay and have the rally replayed.
answers.c: He may give the serving team a verbal warning for minor misconduct.
answers.d: As he has already cautioned the team for this, he penalises this as deliberately screening and also issues a yellow card for unsportsmanlike conduct.
answers.e: He can call the game captain and point out that in the event of a repetition, this will be judged as deliberately screening.

question: Which of the following hand positions of the players of the serving team are allowed at the moment of the service hit?

answers.a: At most one hand is higher than the head.
answers.b: Both hands are clasped behind the head.
answers.c: He rests one hand on his head and leaves the other hand hanging.
answers.d: One hand rests on the head, the other hand is placed on the head (palm facing forwards).

question: In the serving team, the players in positions 2, 3 and 4 have their hands below head height, while the player in position 5 has his hands above head height. The ball is played over position 2 to the opponent. Is this in accordance with the rules?

answers.a: Yes, because the service does not fly over the player concerned and therefore the flight path of the ball is not hidden.
answers.b: Yes, in any case.
answers.c: Non.

question: The ball is passed by the libero with overhand fingers, the attacker hits the ball above net height. This is permitted if the libero, while setting the ball, is located, ...

answers.a: with both feet in the front zone, and the attacker jumps off at least behind the attack line.
answers.b: with one foot in the front zone and with the other foot in the back zone, and the attack is executed within the front zone.
answers.c: with one foot in the back zone and with the other foot in the front zone, while at the moment of the setting action, he lifts his foot into the air in the front zone and thus no longer has contact with the floor, and the attacker jumps off at least behind the attack line.
answers.d: with both feet in the back zone, and the attack is executed within the front zone by a front-row player.

question: Team A's attacker hits the ball into the top band of the net with his third touch, after which the ball is touched a fourth time by team A without an opposing player having touched the ball. The referee shows the following hand signal to signal the fault:

answers.a: Double contact (hand signal #17), if the third and fourth touches are made by the same player.
answers.b: Attack hit fault (hand signal #21).
answers.c: Four hits (hand signal #18), if the third and fourth touches are made by different players.
answers.d: Four hits (hand signal #18), if the third and fourth touches are made by the same player.
